🌸 Turapova Sevinch09/04/2026, 04:56:47
Some people think dangerous sports should be banned, while others believe people should be free to choose. This essay will discuss both views and give my opinion. On the one hand, dangerous sports can be very risky and may cause serious injurys. Activities like skydiving or rock climbing can sometimes lead to accidens or even death. Governments should care about peoples safety and try to reduce danger. In addition, medical treatment can be very expencive, so it can create problems for the healthcare system. Because of this, some people think dangerous sports should not be allowed. On the other hand, many people believe that everyone should have freedom of choice. Doing extreme sports can help people feel more confident and brave. It also gives an exiting experience and strong emotions. Moreover, today there is better equipment and training, which helps reduce risks. Therefore, it may not be fair to ban these sports completely. In my opinion, dangerous sports should not be totally banned because people have the right to choose what they want to do. However, there should be clear rules and safety control to make these activities less dangerous.
Word count: 190

🌸 Turapova Sevinch09/04/2026, 04:55:16
The pie charts illustrate the percentages of five categories of books sold by a bookseller in 1972, 1992 and 2012.Overall, adult fiction became increasingly popular over the period and eventually dominated sales in 2012. In contrast, biography and other books declined noticeably. Meanwhile, children’s fiction showed a gradual increase, whereas travel books fluctuated slightly before falling at the end of the period. In 1972, the category labelled others accounted for the largest share of sales at 25%. Adult fiction, children’s fiction and biography were equally popular, each representing 20% of total sales. Travel books were the least popular category, making up only 15%.Twenty years later, adult fiction rose to 25%, becoming the most popular genre. Children’s fiction also experienced a small increase to 22%. By contrast, biography fell moderately to 15%. Travel books showed a slight rise to 18%, while the proportion of others decreased to 20%. By 2012, adult fiction had grown dramatically to 45%, which was significantly higher than any other category. Children’s fiction continued its upward trend, reaching 25%. However, biography experienced a sharp decline to just 8%, making it the least popular genre. Travel books also fell to 10%, and others dropped considerably to 12%.
Word count: 199

🌸 Dilnura09/04/2026, 03:50:00
Some people think that risky sports should not be allowed, whereas others claim it is personal freedom. This essay will look into both views and explains why I believe that individuals should decide for themselves. On the one hand, there is a belief that impose ban on risky sports can be reasonable. Firstly, these activities often involves a high risk of serious injuries or even death. Sports such is rock climbing or motor racing can lead to serious accidents despite safety tools. For instance, a survey was taken in India which showed than 65% of players injured while doing extreme sports like bunje jumping and swimming. Secondly, risky sports can have a nagitive impact on families. When person is seriously injured, their families may struggle financially and face emotionally stress. This not only effects individuals themselves but also creates problems people around them. Despite these arguments, I believe that people should have the freedom to make their own choices. Initially, while some players play such games they could enhance their mental health and stress levels which can be beneficial for their overall health. For example, they can improve their decision-making skills in hard times because they may face problems while doing risky sports. Additionally, they can build their confidence while they are doing regular basis. In conclusion, while impose ban on dangerous sports may reduce risks and protect public health, it also limits personal freedom. In my view, everyone should have the rights to make their own choices about the activities they enjoy.
Word count: 253

🌸 Farida09/04/2026, 03:21:50
Some people think that dangerous sports should be banned, while others think people should be free to choose. Discuss both views and give your opinion. Some people claim that extreme sports should be completely banned, while another group of people, including myself think that every person should have their own choices. In this essay I will discuss both of these views and explain why I agree with latter opinion. On the one hand, dangerous sports such as base jumping, boxing, skydiving can cause many injuries and even could lead to death. Since not only people who are professionals but also who is inexperienced still take part in this activities. This leads to many accidents even with precautions. As well as that they think injuries from dangerous sports require expensive medical treatment. Therefore, it creates burden on hospitals and emergency services. During risky operations such as helping people who cannot come down from mountain many rescuers also sacrifice their lives because no one can guarantee their safety. On the other hand, every individual should have personal freedom as well as rights to decide how to live their lives. Taking risks is part of human nature, since many people accept risks in many other areas of their livers like driving or traveling so sport should not be the exception. There are also many benefits of extrema sports not only physical which include fitness and healthy lifestyle but also mental, which help in dealing with stress. As well as mental and physical advantage it also benefits economically. Adventure tourism is major source of income in many countries, which also create many job opportunities like guides, instructors and equipment sellers. To conclude, even though extream sports could seem really bad for public because of injuries or pressure on healthcare, which it causes. I as well as many other people think that it has far more benefits than downsides.
Word count: 314
AI Feedback

🌸 Maqsudjon08/04/2026, 22:44:03
task 1 The pie charts illustrate the percentage of five types of books sold by a bookseller in three separate years: 1972, 1992, and 2012. Overall, the most notable change was the sharp rise in adult fiction sales, which became the dominant category by 2012. In contrast, the share of children’s fiction and biography fell considerably over the period. In 1972, adult fiction accounted for 15% of total sales, and this figure surged to 45% in 2012, becoming the largest category. Similarly, travel books increased from 18% in 1972 to 25% in 1992, before declining slightly to 20% in 2012. By comparison, the proportion of children’s fiction, which stood at 20% in 1972, dropped dramatically to 8% in 1992 and then to only 5% in 2012. Biography also saw a decrease, from 20% to 10% over the 40-year period. Expenses on “others”, however, remained relatively stable, at around 25–22%.
Word count: 149
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 7.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Overview:** The introduction provides a concise overview of the main trends, highlighting the rise of adult fiction and the decline of children's fiction and biography.
2. **Data Presentation:** The response effectively summarizes the data with specific figures, making it easy for the reader to understand the changes over the years.
3. **Comparative Analysis:** The use of comparative language (e.g., "by comparison," "similarly") helps to clarify the relationships between different categories and their changes over time.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Limited Detail on All Categories:** While the focus on adult fiction is appropriate, other categories could be explored in more detail. For instance, the category of "others" is mentioned but not elaborated upon.
2. **Lack of Cohesion in Structure:** The organization could be improved by grouping similar data together or by discussing each category in a more structured manner rather than jumping between categories.
3. **Minor Language Issues:** There are a few instances of awkward phrasing, such as "the share of children’s fiction and biography fell considerably," which could be more precise.

**Improvements:**
1. **Expand on All Categories:** Include more detailed analysis of all categories, especially the "others" category, to provide a more comprehensive view of the data.
2. **Enhance Cohesion:** Consider organizing the response by discussing each category in a systematic way, perhaps starting with the category that saw the most significant change and then moving to others.
3. **Refine Language Use:** Improve phrasing for clarity and precision. For example, instead of saying "the share of children’s fiction and biography fell considerably," you could say, "the proportion of children's fiction and biography experienced significant declines."

Overall, this is a well-structured response with clear data representation, but it could benefit from a more thorough exploration of all categories and improved cohesion.
🌸

🌸 Maqsudjon08/04/2026, 22:42:30
Some people argue that dangerous sports should be prohibited by law, while others believe that individuals have the right to participate in them freely. This essay will discuss both perspectives before concluding that personal freedom should prevail, provided that participants are fully informed of the risks. On the one hand, banning hazardous sports can protect people from serious injuries or even death. For example, activities such as base jumping or boxing often lead to long-term physical damage, which places an unnecessary burden on public healthcare systems. Furthermore, young or inexperienced participants may not fully understand the consequences of their choices. A legal ban, therefore, would act as a safeguard for vulnerable individuals and reduce social costs. On the other hand, freedom of choice is a fundamental principle in many societies. Adults should be allowed to assess risks themselves and decide whether to engage in extreme sports. Taking part in such activities can bring personal satisfaction, build mental strength, and even promote tourism and sports-related industries. Moreover, banning dangerous sports might set a problematic precedent, leading to further restrictions on other high-risk activities like skiing or motor racing. Instead of a ban, education and safety regulations would be more reasonable solutions. In my opinion, while governments have a duty to protect citizens, banning dangerous sports is an overreaction. As long as participants are fully aware of the risks and take necessary precautions, they should be free to choose. In conclusion, personal responsibility and informed consent are more important than a complete prohibition.
Word count: 251
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 7.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Structure:** The essay has a clear introduction, body paragraphs that discuss both sides of the argument, and a well-defined conclusion. This organization helps the reader follow the argument easily.
2. **Balanced Argumentation:** The essay presents both perspectives on the issue, which demonstrates an understanding of the complexity of the topic. This is a key requirement for IELTS Task 2.
3. **Use of Examples:** The inclusion of specific examples (base jumping, boxing) strengthens the argument and illustrates the points made.
4. **Cohesion and Coherence:** The use of linking words and phrases (e.g., "On the one hand," "On the other hand," "In my opinion") aids in the flow of the essay and helps connect ideas logically.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Limited Depth of Analysis:** While both sides are presented, the analysis could be deeper. For instance, the potential psychological effects of banning dangerous sports could be explored further.
2. **Repetitive Language:** Some phrases and ideas are repeated, such as the notion of personal freedom and informed consent. More varied vocabulary and expressions could enhance the essay.
3. **Minor Grammatical Errors:** There are a few minor grammatical issues, such as "which places an unnecessary burden" could be better phrased as "which can place an unnecessary burden" to indicate potentiality.

**Improvements:**
1. **Enhance Depth of Argument:** To improve the essay, consider elaborating on the implications of banning dangerous sports, such as the impact on mental health or the cultural significance of these sports in certain societies.
2. **Vary Vocabulary and Sentence Structures:** Use a wider range of vocabulary and sentence structures to avoid repetition. For example, instead of repeatedly using "ban," consider synonyms like "prohibition" or "forbidding."
3. **Proofread for Minor Errors:** Take a moment to proofread the essay for any minor grammatical errors or awkward phrasing to ensure clarity and precision.

Overall, this essay effectively addresses the prompt and presents a well-reasoned argument. With some enhancements in depth and language variety, it could achieve an even higher score.
🌸

🌸 Ozodbek08/04/2026, 12:55:55
Nowadays, many people argue that extreme sports should be banned, while others believe that everyone has their own interests and choices. I partly agree with this trend.  On the one hand, dangerous sports, which are regarded as having the highest risk of death, have a negative impact on people's lives. These days, mostly teenagers are more likely to engage in challenging sports, which can lead to serious issues such as healthcare costs. The first time activities can be at the same time exciting and dangerous. When someone takes part in a particular sport, the risk of injury increases. During this time, participants may have a heart attack, which can be a reason to spend money for health improvements, or, in the worst case, they may die.  However, there are two main reasons why government should not ban extreme sports. Initially, most of the income comes from the hospitals. Accidents on the roads or in sports—all the people from this place commute to the hospital with the aim of checking their health. Secondly, if the government makes a strict law against this type of sport, people would not have a personal choice, which is also the case to be a bigger problem in the future. When people got used to living in the world of freedom, for them it would be difficult to move to new rules. As a result, from the small spot, people may be dissatisfied.  To conclude, by making stricter laws or banning the extreme activities, the government will not have a benefit. In my point of view, people should have their personal choice and control their emotions by making a mistake that is not changeable.
Word count: 278
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Position:** The essay presents a clear stance on the topic, indicating a partial agreement with the idea that extreme sports should not be banned.
2. **Structure:** The essay is organized into distinct paragraphs, which helps in presenting ideas clearly.
3. **Relevant Arguments:** The writer provides relevant arguments for both sides of the debate, demonstrating an understanding of the complexities of the issue.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Grammar and Sentence Structure:** There are several grammatical errors and awkward sentence constructions that hinder clarity. For example, "the first time activities can be at the same time exciting and dangerous" is unclear and confusing.
2. **Development of Ideas:** Some points lack sufficient elaboration. For instance, the argument about hospitals benefiting from extreme sports needs clearer explanation and evidence.
3. **Cohesion and Coherence:** The transitions between ideas are sometimes abrupt, making it difficult to follow the writer's line of reasoning. Phrases like "the case to be a bigger problem" are unclear and disrupt the flow.
4. **Vocabulary:** While the vocabulary is adequate, it lacks variety and sophistication. More precise word choices could enhance the overall quality of the writing.

**Improvements:**
1. **Grammar and Clarity:** Focus on improving grammatical accuracy and sentence structure. Consider revising sentences for clarity and coherence. For example, rephrase awkward constructions to ensure they convey the intended meaning.
2. **Elaboration of Arguments:** Provide more detailed explanations and examples to support your arguments. For instance, elaborate on how extreme sports contribute to hospital income and discuss the implications of personal choice in more depth.
3. **Cohesion:** Use linking words and phrases to improve the flow of ideas. This will help the reader follow your argument more easily. For example, use "Furthermore," "In addition," or "On the other hand" to connect ideas smoothly.
4. **Vocabulary Enhancement:** Work on expanding your vocabulary to express ideas more precisely and effectively. This can help convey your points more convincingly and demonstrate a higher level of language proficiency.

By addressing these areas, you can improve the clarity, coherence, and overall impact of your writing, potentially raising your band score in future assessments.
🌸

🌸 Muslimbek task 208/04/2026, 10:41:29
Some people hold the view that extreme sports should be prohibited, while others claim that there ought to be personal liberty when choosing dangerous activity. This essay intends to discuss both of these claims and explain why I subscribe to the former. On the one hand, there are some reasons why many individuals believe that people should decide whether to engage in dangerous sports. Firstly, they think that many people who take up extreme sports, such as paragliding and BASE jumping, are aware of risks involved. For instance, in skydiving, instructor always tells about all risks involved and what to do in dangerous situations. Secondly, many people would argue that dangerous activities are not as risky as some regular activities. They might think that there are more deaths caused by road accidents, but no one is going to impose ban on roads or cars. Moreover, advocates of this claim think that extreme sports give people the opportunity to challenge themselves mentally and physically, and therefore, banning them is unessesary. Inspire of these arguments, there are some people, including myself, who argue that extreme activities should be banned. A major problem of dangerous sports is far too much advertisement. Dangerous activities are becoming popular today because of publicity it gains compared with safe types of sports which has many benefits, such as fortifiying immune system. Furthermore, our health is our greatest asset, and sport types which put it at threat must be banned. In my view, the best way would be strickter rules, sensible precautions and good equipment could help to avoid the probability of injuries. To sum up, whereas there are some who believe that people should decided themselves whether to take up extreme sports or not, there are some who argue that the best way to prevent injuries is to impose bans and stricter legislation.
Word count: 306
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Structure:** The essay has a clear introduction, body paragraphs discussing both sides of the argument, and a conclusion. This organization helps the reader follow your line of reasoning.
2. **Relevant Arguments:** You present relevant arguments for both sides of the debate regarding extreme sports, demonstrating an understanding of the topic.
3. **Personal Opinion:** You clearly state your opinion in the introduction and conclusion, which is essential for Task 2.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Grammar and Syntax Errors:** There are several grammatical mistakes, such as “instructor always tells” (should be “the instructor always tells”), “unessesary” (should be “unnecessary”), and “decided themselves” (should be “decide for themselves”). These errors can disrupt the flow and clarity of your writing.
2. **Vocabulary Use:** Some word choices are awkward or incorrect, such as “fortifiying” (should be “fortifying”) and “strickter” (should be “stricter”). Additionally, phrases like “the best way would be” could be more effectively expressed.
3. **Clarity and Cohesion:** Some sentences are unclear or too complex, which can confuse the reader. For instance, “A major problem of dangerous sports is far too much advertisement” could be rephrased for clarity.

**Improvements:**
1. **Proofreading:** Spend time proofreading your essay to catch grammatical errors and typos. This will improve the overall quality of your writing.
2. **Variety in Sentence Structure:** Use a variety of sentence structures to enhance readability. Mixing simple and complex sentences can make your writing more engaging.
3. **Expand on Ideas:** Some points could be elaborated further. For example, you could provide more specific examples or statistics to support your claims about the dangers of extreme sports.
4. **Use of Linking Words:** Incorporate more cohesive devices (e.g., “however,” “furthermore,” “in addition”) to improve the flow of your arguments and enhance the logical connection between ideas.

By focusing on these areas for improvement, you can enhance the clarity, coherence, and overall quality of your writing, potentially achieving a higher band score in the IELTS Writing Task.

Data on the proportion of weekly earnings by households in a country in the years 1968 and 2018 is compared in the bar chart provided. Overall significant changes occurred between 1968 and 2018, with noticeable increases in categories such as leisure and housing. While food and personal goods cost shows considerable decrease over the period. In 1968, households spent highest proportion of their expenditure for food, accounting for nearly 35% of their income. However, in 2018, this figure dropped dramatically to about 17%. Similarly, figures for feul and power, clothing and footwear, personal goods all declined over the time. All of these categories made up small proportions in 2018, less than 5%. Turning to the second categories, housing expenditure increased noticeably, accounted for about 20% in 2018. Spending on transport also rose, from abour 8% to 15%. Household goods accouted for more than 5% of weekly income in both years. In 2018, spending on leisure was almost twice as high as in 1968.

🌸 Ozodbek04/04/2026, 06:32:13
The given bar chart illustrates how families in one country spent their weekly income in 1968 and 2018. Overall, it can be seen that in 1968 people spent the largest proportion of their money on food, while in 2018 leisure and housing became more popular. In addition, spending on some categories decreased, whereas others increased over time. Looking at the information in more detail, food accounted for the biggest share in 1968 at around 35%, but this figure dropped significantly to about 15% in 2018. Similarly, spending on clothing and footwear decreased from approximately 10% to about 5%. Fuel and power also saw a slight decline. However, other categories showed an opposite trend. Housing increased from around 10% to nearly 20% in 2018. Transport also rose noticeably, reaching about 15%. The most significant growth can be seen in leisure, which increased from around 9% to over 20%, becoming one of the highest categories in 2018.
Word count: 155
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 7**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Overview:** The introduction provides a clear overview of the main trends in the data, effectively summarizing the key points of comparison between 1968 and 2018.
2. **Logical Structure:** The response is well-organized, with a clear progression from the general trends to specific details. The use of paragraphs helps in maintaining clarity.
3. **Use of Data:** The candidate effectively uses specific data points to support their observations, which enhances the credibility of the analysis.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Limited Vocabulary:** While the vocabulary used is adequate, it lacks variety and sophistication. Phrases like "increased," "decreased," and "saw a slight decline" are repeated, which can make the writing feel monotonous.
2. **Lack of Comparison:** Although the response mentions changes over time, it could benefit from more comparative language to highlight the differences more vividly (e.g., "in contrast to" or "compared to").
3. **Minor Grammatical Errors:** There are a few minor grammatical inaccuracies, such as "the biggest share in 1968 at around 35%" which could be more clearly stated as "the biggest share of spending in 1968, accounting for around 35%."

**Improvements:**
1. **Enhance Vocabulary:** Incorporate a wider range of vocabulary and phrases to describe trends and comparisons. For example, use synonyms for "increase" and "decrease" such as "rise," "surge," "fall," or "decline."
2. **Include More Comparisons:** Use more comparative phrases to emphasize the differences between the two years. For instance, "In contrast to the significant drop in food expenditure, leisure spending saw a remarkable rise."
3. **Check for Clarity and Precision:** Revise sentences for clarity and ensure that all data points are clearly articulated. For example, clarify the context for "fuel and power" by specifying that it also refers to household expenses.

By focusing on these areas, the candidate can enhance the quality of their writing and potentially achieve a higher band score.

🌸 Maqsudjon04/04/2026, 05:51:23
The bar chart compares how the average weekly income of families in a particular country was distributed across eight categories in 1968 and 2018. Overall, the largest proportion of spending shifted from food in 1968 to leisure in 2018. While expenditure on housing, transport, and leisure rose significantly, spending on food, clothing, fuel, and personal goods fell. Household goods remained unchanged. In 1968, an average household allocated the biggest share of their weekly income to food (35%). By 2018, this figure had dropped dramatically to around 17%. Similarly, spending on clothing and footwear fell from approximately 10% to 5%, while expenses on fuel and power and personal goods also decreased moderately. Conversely, leisure saw the most notable rise, increasing from under 10% in 1968 to over 20% in 2018, becoming the largest category. Housing expenditure nearly doubled from about 10% to roughly 18%, and transport rose from roughly 8% to 14%. Household goods, which was the only category experiencing almost no change, stood at about 8% in both years.

The bar chart illustrates how families in one country spent their weekly income in 1968 and 2018 on different categories. Overall, it can be clearly seen that food was the biggest expense in 1968, while in 2018 people spent more money on leisure and housing. In addition, spending on clothing and footwear, personal goods and fuel and power decreased over time. In 1968, families spent about 35% of their income on food, which was much higher than any other category. However, in 2018 this figure fell significantly to around 17%. By contrast, spending on leisure increased noticeably from approximately 9% to about 22%, becoming one of the highest proportions.Housing also showed a considerable rise, growing from nearly 10% in 1968 to almost 19% in 2018. Similarly, the percentage of money spent on transport went up from about 8% to roughly 14%. On the other hand, several categories experienced a decline. For example, expenditure on clothing and footwear dropped from around 10% to 5%. Likewise, spending on personal goods and household goods became lower. Fuel and power also slightly decreased. In conclusion, families changed their spending habits over the period, prefering to spend more on housing and leisure, while less money was used for basic needs such as food and clothing.
Word count: 210
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 7**

**Strengths:**
1. **Overview and Structure:** The response provides a clear overview of the main trends in the bar chart, effectively summarizing the key changes in spending habits between 1968 and 2018.
2. **Data Interpretation:** The candidate accurately interprets the data, providing specific percentages to support their observations. This demonstrates a good understanding of the information presented.
3. **Cohesion and Coherence:** The essay is well-organized, with logical progression from the introduction to the body paragraphs and conclusion. The use of linking words and phrases helps to guide the reader through the information.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Detail and Specificity:** While the candidate mentions several categories, some details are vague. For instance, the phrase "spending on personal goods and household goods became lower" lacks specific data, which would strengthen the analysis.
2. **Language Variety:** The vocabulary used is somewhat repetitive. Phrases like "spent more money" and "spending habits" could be varied to demonstrate a wider range of language proficiency.
3. **Minor Errors:** There are a few grammatical and typographical errors, such as "prefering" instead of "preferring," and the phrase "became one of the highest proportions" could be rephrased for clarity.

**Improvements:**
1. **Include Specific Data:** When discussing categories that experienced a decline, provide specific percentages to enhance the analysis. For example, state the exact figures for personal goods and household goods.
2. **Vary Vocabulary:** Use synonyms and varied expressions to avoid repetition. For example, instead of "spending," consider using "expenditure" or "allocation of funds."
3. **Proofread for Errors:** Take a moment to check for spelling and grammatical errors. This will help improve the overall quality of the writing and ensure clarity.

By addressing these weaknesses and incorporating the suggested improvements, the candidate could aim for a higher band score in future writing tasks.

Data on how households allocated their weekly earnings across various categories in the years 1968 and 2018 is compared in the graph provided. Overall, it can be concluded from the chart that food and leisure were leading categories in 1968 and 2018, respectively; the figures for housing and transport experienced the most significant changes, while those for household goods remained stable throughout the whole period. To begin, both numbers for food, the category which topped the list in 1968, and clothing and footware were accounted for 35% and a tenth, acordingly. The figures for the former underwent a considerable decline of approximately 18%, while those for the latter decreased by a half. The percentage of weekly money spent by families on personal goods in 1968, stood at about 8% and experienced moderate fall of 4%, whereas the proportion of weekly salary allocated for fuel and power constituted about 6% and fell by small minority. Looking further, the proportion of median spending on housing by domestic units was accounted for 10% of weekly expanditures and rose considerably by approximately 11% and that for leisure activities, the category which saw the bigest rise and became a leading category, increased from 9% to about a fifth between 1968 and 2018. AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Task Achievement:** The response addresses the task by providing an overview of the data and comparing the key categories of household expenditure in 1968 and 2018.
2. **Coherence and Cohesion:** The essay is structured logically, with clear paragraphs that separate different aspects of the data.
3. **Lexical Resource:** There is a good range of vocabulary related to the topic, such as "allocated," "proportion," "decline," and "expenditures."

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Grammar and Sentence Structure:** There are several grammatical errors and awkward sentence constructions that impede clarity. For example, "the category which topped the list in 1968, and clothing and footware were accounted for 35% and a tenth, acordingly" is confusing and contains errors.
2. **Accuracy of Data Representation:** There are inaccuracies in the representation of the data, such as "the percentage of weekly money spent by families on personal goods in 1968, stood at about 8% and experienced moderate fall of 4%." This could be clearer and more precise.
3. **Punctuation and Spelling:** There are spelling mistakes (e.g., "acordingly," "bigest," "within") and punctuation issues that detract from the overall quality of the writing.

**Improvements:**
1. **Grammar and Clarity:** Focus on improving grammatical accuracy and sentence structure. For instance, instead of "the category which topped the list in 1968, and clothing and footware were accounted for 35% and a tenth, acordingly," you could say, "In 1968, food was the leading category, accounting for 35% of expenditures, while clothing and footwear represented 10%."

2. **Data Accuracy:** Ensure that all data points are accurately represented and clearly explained. Use precise language to describe changes in percentages and avoid vague terms like "small minority."

3. **Proofreading:** Spend time proofreading your work to catch spelling and punctuation errors. This will enhance the professionalism of your writing and improve readability.

4. **Variety in Sentence Structure:** Incorporate a variety of sentence structures to enhance the flow of your writing. This can help maintain reader interest and demonstrate a higher level of language proficiency.

By addressing these areas, you can improve your writing and potentially achieve a higher band score in the IELTS Writing Task.

🌸 Maqsudjon31/03/2026, 05:33:34
Cybercrime is growing fast because many people now use the internet to make financial transactions. There are several steps that governments and individuals can take to tackle this problem.
Regarding government actions, authorities need to be tough on crime by introducing stricter rules against online criminals. If people are found guilty of internet fraud, serious punishments should be given as this will act as a deterrent and stop others from doing the same. Police should also receive more power to arrest suspects and enforce the law effectively. In addition, governments must take into consideration the importance of running public awareness campaigns so that citizens do not turn to unsafe websites when making payments online.
On the other hand, banks and technology companies also have a big responsibility. They should install surveillance cameras and stronger security systems to detect suspicious activities. Using forensic evidence such as digital records can help authorities convict criminals who steal financial information. Moreover, companies that handle customer money should give a chance of rehabilitation to minor offenders by putting them on probation, which helps them reintegrate back into society rather than becoming repeat criminals.
In conclusion, reducing internet crime requires cooperation between governments, law enforcement, and private companies. By enforcing stronger laws and improving security technology, it is possible to make online financial transactions much safer for everyone.
Word count: 222
AI Feedback
🌸

🌸 Dilnura31/03/2026, 04:27:44
Internet crime is increasing rapidly as more and more people are using the internet to make financial transactions. What can be done to tackle this problem? Nowadays, online crime is growing quicly due to increasing number of people making online payment. Cybercrime is dangerous when we take into consideration that many users access their bank account online. This essay will discuss several effective measures to reduce cybercrime. One possible solution is improve awareness among users about online risks. Many people are not properly educated about cyber securities. For example, people should avoid opening suspecious links or files so that they can protect their personal information. Some individuals tend to use weak passwords or share personal information on unreliable websites. As a result, hackers can easily access thir account or steal their data. To tackle this issue, governments should focus on raising public awareness by training programs. By teaching people how to recognize online threats and protect their information, the number of cybercrime can be reduced. Another way to reduce internet crime is to careful monitoring and reporting suspicious activities. People and companies should regularly check their bank statements and online accounts. If they notice something strange, they should report it immediately to the bank or authorities. People should install more professional passwords with the aim of protecting their personal accounts from casual hackers. Online companies can also use programs that observe unusual activities, for fear that criminals may steal data. By acting quicly they can stop criminals before they do serious damage. In conclusion, internet crime is rising because many people are not careful online, but it can reduced by learning safe habits and monitoring accounts carefully.
Word count: 277
AI Feedback

There are several ways to solve this problem, which include using stronger passwords and raicing awareness and educating public about it. One of the ways to tackle this problem is using more professional passwords. As many people recommend adding numbers to password so casual hackers cannot easily find out about it. Government could invite laws about educating about viruses and malware with the aim of protecting public from hackers. 🌸 Ozodbek29/03/2026, 12:21:13
As the number of internet users is growing significantly, online fraud is one of the most common crimes that many people face during online transactions. There are many feasible actions that can be handled to tackle this issue.  One possible reason for this trend is that many people are not aware of online threats. In this modern world, where technology becomes a part of life, Gen X is less familiar with technology. If we took elders who were born until 1980 as an example, many older people are not into technology, while the modern generation cannot live without the internet. As a result, they do not have knowledge about online security or how to protect their data in their bank accounts by using only the internet and phone. In addition, between parents and teenagers, parents are more likely to be a victim of online crime.  However, there are many solutions for this trend, and one of them is teaching from a younger age. Schools or parents should teach their children digital literacy with the aim of making them aware of online fraud. When teachers teach their students something new, the brains of the students are fresh, and they may absorb everything like a sponge. Another solution to enhancing security systems. These days the portion of hackers who are stealing money from bank accounts is increasing. To be ready for this type of crime, people have to make stronger passwords lest they become victims of cybercrime, or banks should introduce two-factor authentication.  To conclude, although internet crime is becoming more common, it can be reduced if people are educated about online risks and if stronger security systems are implemented.
Word count: 278
AI Feedback
**IELTS Writing Task Feedback**

**Band Score: 6.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Structure:** The essay has a clear introduction, body paragraphs, and conclusion, which helps in guiding the reader through your argument.
2. **Relevant Ideas:** You present relevant ideas regarding the causes of online fraud and potential solutions, demonstrating a good understanding of the topic.
3. **Use of Examples:** The use of generational examples (Gen X and younger generations) effectively illustrates your point about differing levels of technological awareness.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Language and Grammar:** There are some grammatical errors and awkward phrases that hinder clarity. For example, "where technology becomes a part of life" could be more clearly expressed as "where technology has become an integral part of life."
2. **Coherence and Cohesion:** Some sentences are not well connected, making it difficult for the reader to follow your arguments smoothly. For instance, the transition between discussing the lack of awareness and the proposed solutions could be more fluid.
3. **Depth of Analysis:** While you mention several solutions, they could be elaborated further. For example, discussing how digital literacy can be integrated into school curriculums would strengthen your argument.

**Improvements:**
1. **Grammar and Vocabulary:** Review your essay for grammatical accuracy and consider varying your vocabulary to avoid repetition. For instance, instead of repeatedly using "online," you could use synonyms like "digital" or "cyber."
2. **Enhance Cohesion:** Use linking words and phrases (e.g., "Furthermore," "Moreover," "On the other hand") to improve the flow between sentences and paragraphs.
3. **Expand on Solutions:** Provide more detailed explanations or examples of how the proposed solutions could be implemented. For instance, you could discuss specific programs or initiatives that schools could adopt to teach digital literacy.
4. **Clarify Ideas:** Ensure that each point is clearly explained. For example, clarify how two-factor authentication works and why it is effective in preventing online fraud.

By addressing these areas, you can enhance the clarity, coherence, and overall quality of your writing, potentially achieving a higher band score in future assessments.

🌸 Muslimbek task 228/03/2026, 23:58:20
Recently, since many people are using online platforms in order to make monetary exchanges, digital malfeasance has become a widespread issue. Despite the seriousness of the problem, I believe that this issue can be tackled in a number of ways. The most efficient way to address this problem is improving bank systems. In order to protect people from casual hackers, banks could ask biometric data, such as fingerprints or facial recognition which are impossible to replicate, instead of one-time codes sent to a physical device. For example, if bank applications asked biometric data for logging into account, scammers could not deceive people and ask for one-time code for logging into their accounts and steal money. Moreover, banks could utilize AI driven apps so as to detect suspicious account and freeze them, and detect and prevent fraught. Therefore, there are many ways how banks could prevent fraught in order to protect not only money , but also personal data of their clients. Another effective measure to tackle this problem is public action. Stronger and more proffesional password should be used by people for the fear that casual hackers would steal their money and gain their personal data. Furthermore, people must avoid opening suspicious links or files sent by relatives or friends in order that they could prevent hackers from stealing their personal data and registrating unwanted loans. Schools also could teach children how to install antivirus softwares so as to protect their computer at home. Thus, strong passwords and education could prevent people from installing malware programmes. To sum up, although the situation is serious, sensible and well-designed solutions have the potential to improve conditions considerably.
Word count: 275
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Structure:** The essay has a clear introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion. Each paragraph discusses a specific solution to the problem, which helps in maintaining coherence.
2. **Relevant Ideas:** The suggestions provided for tackling digital malfeasance are relevant and demonstrate an understanding of the issue.
3. **Use of Examples:** The use of examples, such as biometric data and AI-driven apps, adds depth to the arguments and supports the points made.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Language Errors:** There are several grammatical and spelling mistakes, such as "proffesional" (should be "professional"), "registrating" (should be "registering"), and "fraught" (should be "fraud"). These errors can distract the reader and affect clarity.
2. **Complexity of Ideas:** Some sentences are overly complex and could be simplified for better readability. For instance, the sentence about banks using AI could be broken down for clarity.
3. **Lack of Development in Ideas:** While the suggestions are relevant, they could be further developed. For example, elaborating on how biometric data is implemented or discussing potential challenges could strengthen the argument.

**Improvements:**
1. **Proofreading:** Take time to proofread the essay for spelling and grammatical errors. This will help improve the overall quality of writing.
2. **Simplify Sentence Structure:** Aim for clearer, more concise sentences. Avoid overly complex structures that may confuse the reader.
3. **Expand on Ideas:** Provide more detailed explanations or examples for each solution. Discuss potential drawbacks or challenges to give a more balanced view.
4. **Use of Vocabulary:** Incorporate a wider range of vocabulary to enhance the essay and demonstrate language proficiency. Avoid repetition of words and phrases.

By addressing these weaknesses and focusing on improvements, the essay could achieve a higher band score in future assessments.

🌸 Farida28/03/2026, 05:44:41
Some people think that poverty is the reason behind most crimes. Do you agree or disagree? Nowadays it is increasingly common for people to believe that majority of crimes are committed because of poverty. I partly agree with this view, since there are also other factors like alcohol, drugs and social circle, which forces people to commit crimes. On the one hand, some criminals commit crimes like shoplifting or burglary because of financial pressure. Therefore, for them it is an easier way to meet basic needs like housing, pricy medicine and food for subsistence. Another reason is that it is more challenging for people from poor families to have same educational opportunities as their middle-class peers. This is because they do not have the same knowledge many of them cannot apply for a grant or pay for their tuition fees. It further leads to problem with finding job prospects and crime could seem like the best option for them. On the other hand, there are plenty of other factors which influence people in turning to crimes. Firstly, many people engage in illegal activities under the influence of alcohol or drugs and do not notice how they influenced by it. Secondly, some individuals lack of moral values or have a poor upbringing. Criminal behavior can stem from family background, since many criminals could be influenced by not only their social circle but family members. To sum up, we cannot blame only poor people in turning to crimes because they need money for survival. As there also many reasons why people turn to crimes, such as under influence of alcohol, drugs or even by relatives.
Word count: 273
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Position:** The essay clearly states a position, indicating partial agreement with the statement that poverty is a reason behind most crimes.
2. **Structure:** The essay is well-structured with a clear introduction, body paragraphs discussing both sides of the argument, and a conclusion.
3. **Relevant Examples:** The writer provides relevant examples to support the points made, such as the impact of financial pressure and the influence of alcohol and drugs.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Language and Grammar:** There are several grammatical errors and awkward phrases that detract from the clarity of the writing. For example, "majority of crimes" should be "the majority of crimes," and "lack of moral values" should be "a lack of moral values."
2. **Cohesion and Coherence:** Some sentences are not well-connected, which can make the argument difficult to follow. For instance, the transition between discussing poverty and other factors could be smoother.
3. **Development of Ideas:** While the points made are relevant, they could be developed further. For example, elaborating on how alcohol and drugs specifically lead to crime would strengthen the argument.

**Improvements:**
1. **Grammar and Vocabulary:** Review and practice grammar rules, particularly articles, prepositions, and subject-verb agreement. Expanding vocabulary would also help in expressing ideas more clearly and accurately.
2. **Cohesion:** Use linking words and phrases (e.g., "Moreover," "In addition," "On the contrary") to improve the flow of ideas and make transitions between points clearer.
3. **Elaboration:** Provide more detailed examples or explanations for each point made. For instance, discussing specific types of crimes associated with drug use or providing statistics on crime rates related to poverty could enhance the argument.
4. **Conclusion:** Strengthen the conclusion by summarizing the main points more effectively and reiterating the complexity of the issue without introducing new ideas.

By addressing these weaknesses and implementing the suggested improvements, the essay could potentially achieve a higher band score.
🌸
🌚🌝
🌸 Turapova Sevinch28/03/2026, 05:40:29
It is often argued that poverty is the main cause of criminal behavior. While I understand this point of view, I believe that there are also other significant factors that lead people to commit crimes. This essay will explain why I partly agree with this idea. Poverty can indeed push some individuals towards criminal activities. People living in extreme poverty may struggle to afford basic necessities such as food, clothing, and shelter. In such situations, theft or petty crimes can become a means of survival. Moreover, lack of access to education and job opportunities often limits lawful ways to earn money, which may lead to illegal alternatives. For example, studies show that areas with high unemployment rates often experience higher rates of property crimes. However, poverty is not the only reason behind crimes. Some criminals come from wealthy backgrounds but commit fraud, cybercrime, or violent acts due to greed, psychological problems, or peer pressure. Social environment and upbringing also play a crucial role: individuals exposed to violence or criminal behavior in their families or neighborhoods are more likely to follow similar patterns. This indicates that crime is a complex issue influenced by multiple factors. In conclusion, while poverty can contribute to criminal behavior by limiting opportunities and creating desperation, it is not the sole cause. Psychological, social, and personal factors are equally important in understanding why people commit crimes. Therefore, addressing crime requires a multifaceted approach rather than focusing solely on economic conditions.
Word count: 243
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 7.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Position**: The essay clearly states the writer's position on the topic, indicating partial agreement with the argument that poverty is a main cause of criminal behavior.
2. **Well-Structured**: The essay is logically organized with a clear introduction, body paragraphs that explore different aspects of the argument, and a concise conclusion.
3. **Relevant Examples**: The use of examples, such as the correlation between unemployment and property crimes, adds credibility to the argument and illustrates the points made.
4. **Complex Sentences**: The writer effectively uses a variety of sentence structures, which enhances the overall readability and sophistication of the writing.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Limited Range of Vocabulary**: While the vocabulary used is appropriate, it lacks some variety and sophistication that could enhance the essay. Phrases like "basic necessities" and "means of survival" could be replaced with more varied expressions.
2. **Minor Grammatical Errors**: There are a few minor grammatical issues, such as "some individuals towards criminal activities" where "towards" could be replaced with "into" for better clarity.
3. **Lack of Depth in Examples**: While examples are provided, they could be more detailed or specific to strengthen the argument. For instance, mentioning specific studies or statistics would add depth.

**Improvements:**
1. **Enhance Vocabulary**: Incorporate a wider range of vocabulary and phrases to express ideas more vividly. This could involve using synonyms or more complex expressions related to crime and societal issues.
2. **Address Grammatical Issues**: Review and revise sentences for grammatical accuracy. Paying attention to prepositions and verb forms will improve clarity and precision.
3. **Develop Examples Further**: Provide more detailed examples or case studies to support the argument. This could involve referencing specific research studies or real-world instances that illustrate the points made.
4. **Expand on Counterarguments**: While the essay mentions other factors contributing to crime, it could benefit from a deeper exploration of these factors, perhaps by discussing how they interact with poverty.

By focusing on these areas for improvement, the writer can enhance the overall quality of the essay and potentially achieve a higher band score.

🌸 Ozodbek28/03/2026, 04:14:30
A growing number of people believe that the reason for most crimes is poverty. This essay partly agrees with this perspective because this trend has two sides, depending on the type of person.  On the one hand, when people cannot afford basic necessities, they may turn to crime. These days, many people who do not have particular knowledge about the work, for example, teenagers, are more likely to commit a crime due to lack of job opportunities. In modern life, when you do not have a stable income, it can affect your mental health. As a result, because of depression and the pressure that would come from family, more and more people embark on crime. In addition, if individuals do not have work, they do not have income as well, which is the main reason why people turn to crime.  However, many crimes are committed by wealthy individuals for greed rather than needs. White-collar crimes such as fraud or corruption that are happening nowadays with the view of being more popular took place in the majority of countries, which is why some of them who did not get caught yet have huge reputations in the world of criminals. Furthermore, the surroundings also have a crucial role in this trend. For instance, bad friends or the peer pressure that may come from them might make sense to the personality of people, and finally, they may turn to crime.  To conclude, I cannot agree with the idea that poverty is the main cause of most crimes. Although it can be a reason, many crimes are committed with the purpose of feeding their families or themselves.
Word count: 273
AI Feedback
**IELTS Writing Task Feedback**

**Band Score: 6.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Structure:** The essay has a clear introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion, which helps in organizing your ideas logically.
2. **Balanced Argument:** You present both sides of the argument, acknowledging that poverty can lead to crime while also noting that wealthy individuals can commit crimes out of greed.
3. **Relevant Examples:** The use of examples, such as the impact of mental health and the influence of peers, adds depth to your argument.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Coherence and Cohesion:** Some sentences could be better linked to improve the flow of ideas. For instance, transitions between points could be smoother.
2. **Grammar and Vocabulary:** There are several grammatical errors and awkward phrases that detract from the overall clarity. For example, "the reason for most crimes is poverty" could be rephrased for better clarity.
3. **Depth of Analysis:** While you mention that wealthy individuals commit crimes, the analysis could be deeper. More specific examples of white-collar crimes could enhance your argument.

**Improvements:**
1. **Enhance Coherence:** Use linking words and phrases (e.g., "Moreover," "In addition," "On the contrary") to create smoother transitions between ideas and paragraphs.
2. **Refine Grammar and Vocabulary:** Review your essay for grammatical accuracy. For example, "the view of being more popular took place" is unclear and could be rewritten as "the prevalence of white-collar crimes has increased."
3. **Expand on Examples:** Provide more specific examples of white-collar crimes and their consequences to strengthen your argument. For instance, mention specific cases or statistics to illustrate the prevalence of such crimes.
4. **Clarify Your Conclusion:** Your conclusion could be more definitive. Instead of stating that you "cannot agree," consider rephrasing it to assert your position more strongly, such as "While poverty can contribute to crime, it is essential to recognize that many crimes are driven by greed and social influences."

By addressing these areas, you can improve the clarity and effectiveness of your argument, potentially raising your band score in future essays.

This shows poverty is not only effects daily life but also lead to sudden crimes. In conclusion, poverty is major factor behind many crimes. Adressing poverty and providing better support is issential to reduce crime in society.

**Band Score: 5.5**

**Strengths:**

2. **Relevant Arguments:** The author provides relevant reasons and examples to support the argument, such as limited access to education and the emotional impact of poverty.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Grammar and Syntax Errors:** There are several grammatical mistakes, such as "commented crimes," "necessaties," "the purpose of provide," and "effects daily life." These errors can confuse the reader and detract from the overall clarity of the writing.

By focusing on these areas, the essay can achieve a higher band score and present a more compelling argument.

🌸 Muslimbek task 227/03/2026, 10:51:43
There is a growing belief that financial hardship is the main cause of criminal behavior. This essay intends to partly agree with this claim mainly because while poverty contributes to crime, it is not the sole factor. A major reason why this view is valid is financial desperation. People may turn into crime, such as shoplifting and pickpocketing, in order to earn a living. Another strong idea supporting this claim is lack job opportunities. Many criminals and even ex-convicts embark on crime since they cannot find an appropriate job. Moreover, offenders may have a poor education and therefore, many employers are reluctant to hire them. Thus, I believe that these reasons are the most common factors for poverty and staring to commit crimes. One major reason why this claim is unconvincing is greed and materialism. Not only poor people commit crimes, but also some milioners turn to crimes like fraud and corruption. Another factor that challenges this view is drug and alcohol addiction. Some people who addicted to alcohol may commit crimes like crime of passion or traffic offence because of alcohol intoxine. Furthermore, there are also crime committed by people who have mental problems which are not caused by poverty. Hence, there are other types of crime which are not caused by poverty. To sum up, while some crime types including picpocketing and shoplifting are caused by financial desperation, there are other types like crime of passion and traffic offences are caused by other factors.
Word count: 246
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 5.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Position:** The essay presents a clear position that partially agrees with the statement, indicating an understanding of the task.
2. **Relevant Ideas:** The writer provides relevant reasons and examples to support both sides of the argument, discussing financial hardship and other factors contributing to crime.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Coherence and Cohesion:** The essay lacks clear organization. The ideas are not well-connected, and transitions between points are abrupt, making it hard to follow the argument.
2. **Grammar and Vocabulary:** There are several grammatical errors (e.g., "lack job opportunities" should be "lack of job opportunities," "milioners" should be "millionaires," "intoxine" should be "intoxication"). Vocabulary is somewhat limited and repetitive.
3. **Development of Ideas:** Some points are underdeveloped. For instance, the discussion of greed and drug addiction could be expanded with more examples or explanations.
4. **Conclusion:** The conclusion is weak and does not effectively summarize the main points or reinforce the writer's position.

**Improvements:**
1. **Organize Ideas:** Structure the essay more clearly with distinct paragraphs for each main idea. Use topic sentences to introduce each paragraph and ensure that each paragraph develops a single idea.
2. **Enhance Vocabulary and Grammar:** Work on expanding vocabulary and improving grammatical accuracy. Consider using synonyms and varied sentence structures to make the writing more engaging.
3. **Develop Arguments:** Provide more detailed explanations and examples for each point. For instance, when discussing greed, provide specific examples of crimes committed by wealthy individuals.

By focusing on these areas, the essay can be improved to achieve a higher band score.

🌸 Farida25/03/2026, 02:24:22
The pie charts illustrate the proportions of people aged 23–65 working in different occupations in a UK town called Ashby and in the UK overall in 2008. Overall, it is clear that personal service and office work accounted for the largest shares in both Ashby and the UK. However, Ashby had higher percentages in construction and personal service. In Ashby, the largest proportion of workers was in personal service, at 21%. Office work was the second most common occupation, making 18% of the workforce. Construction work accounted for 16%, while both unemployment and shop work stood at 14% each. Smaller proportions were seen in technical work and professional work. In contrast, in the UK as a whole, office work represented the highest percentage at 19%. Both technical work and personal service each made up 17% of employment. Professional work accounted for 14%, followed by shop work at 13%. The figures for unemployment and construction work were the lowest, both at 10%. In conclude, while the distribution of jobs in Ashby and the UK was broadly similar, Ashby had more workers in manual and service-related roles, whereas the national figures showed a more interest in technical and professional occupations.
Word count: 198

🌸 Dilnura24/03/2026, 04:59:19
Some people think certain prisoners should be made to do unpaid community work instead of being put behind bars. To what extent do you agree? Many people argue that certain offenders should work without pay rather than serving a prison sentence. This essay fully agrees with this perspective because it allows offenders to contribute positively to society and give them change of rehabilitation. One key reason why this view is valid is that community work can be benificial for minor offenders. Due to strict rules, even minor crime such as simple fighting can lead to prison sentence. If such individuals deal with such community works, it can help them reintegrate back into society. For example, instead of sent to prison, they can do public works such as clean public spaces, which allows them learn responsibility. Unless offenders are dangerous for society, they should be given community work instead of prison. Therefore, taking part in social work not only helps them reintegrate back into community but also allows them contribute positively to society. Another strong argument supporting this idea is that government can save money. Allowing minor criminals to engage in unpaid community work can help government safe a significant amount of money. The cost of maintain offenders in prison is high for food or clothes. Instead of use the money for criminals, these money can be used to important things such as education , technology or healthcare. Community work can be effective to prison as long as offenders are properly supervised. As a result, encouraging minor criminals to participate in community work cab be usefull for both government and community. To sum up, individuals who commit minor crime should be given the opportunity to do community work instead of sent to prison. It can help them to make a fresh start.
Word count: 301
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Position:** The essay clearly states a position in favor of community work over prison sentences for minor offenders, which is essential for Task Achievement.
2. **Relevant Arguments:** The points made about rehabilitation and cost savings are relevant and support the main argument effectively.
3. **Structure:** The essay has a logical structure with an introduction, body paragraphs, and a conclusion, which aids in readability.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Grammar and Spelling Errors:** There are several grammatical mistakes (e.g., "benificial," "safe" instead of "save," "usefull," "sent to prison" should be "sent to prison") that detract from the overall clarity and professionalism of the writing.
2. **Inconsistent Vocabulary:** The vocabulary used is somewhat repetitive and lacks variety. Phrases like "community work" and "minor offenders" are used multiple times without synonyms or paraphrasing.
3. **Development of Ideas:** Some arguments could be further developed. For instance, the idea of rehabilitation could include more examples or explanations on how community work facilitates this process.
4. **Cohesion and Coherence:** While the essay is structured, the transitions between ideas could be smoother. Some sentences feel abrupt or disconnected from the previous thoughts.

**Improvements:**
1. **Proofreading:** Spend time proofreading the essay to catch grammatical errors and typos. This will improve the overall quality of the writing.
2. **Expand Ideas:** Develop each argument further by providing more detailed examples or explanations. This will enhance the depth of the essay and demonstrate critical thinking.
3. **Variety in Language:** Use a wider range of vocabulary and sentence structures to avoid repetition and make the writing more engaging.
4. **Improve Cohesion:** Use linking words and phrases to connect ideas more fluidly. For example, phrases like "In addition," "Furthermore," or "Moreover" can help transition between points.

By addressing these weaknesses and implementing the suggested improvements, the essay could potentially reach a higher band score in future assessments.

Data on the proportion of people aged 23-65 working in various jobs in the UK and in a town Ashby in 2008 is compared in the charts provided. AI Feedback
**IELTS Writing Task Feedback**

**Band Score: 5.5**

**Strengths:**
1. **Overview:** You provided a clear overall summary of the data, highlighting the main trends in both Ashby and the UK.
2. **Data Presentation:** You included specific percentages, which is essential for task achievement and demonstrates an understanding of the data.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Coherence and Cohesion:** The organization of your response could be improved. Some sentences feel disjointed, and the flow of information could be smoother. For instance, the transition between discussing Ashby and the UK is abrupt.
2. **Grammar and Sentence Structure:** There are several grammatical errors and awkward phrases that hinder clarity. For example, "the largest percentage refers to office work" could be more clearly stated as "the largest percentage of workers in the UK were in office work."
3. **Details and Comparisons:** While you provided some comparisons, they were not always clear or well-explained. For instance, stating that "the percentage of employees who work in office was twice as high as that of working technical work" could be more precise.

**Improvements:**
1. **Enhance Coherence:** Use linking words and phrases to improve the flow of your writing. For example, use phrases like "In contrast," "Similarly," or "On the other hand" to connect your ideas more effectively.
2. **Refine Grammar and Vocabulary:** Pay attention to grammar, particularly subject-verb agreement and sentence structure. Consider revising sentences for clarity and conciseness. For example, instead of "the proportion of unemployment and workers in a shops made up 14% each in equal amount," you could say, "both unemployment and shop workers accounted for 14% each."
3. **Expand on Comparisons:** Provide more detailed comparisons and explanations. Instead of simply stating percentages, explain what they imply about the job market in both Ashby and the UK. For example, you could discuss why personal service might be more prevalent in Ashby compared to the UK.

**Overall Recommendation:** Focus on improving the organization of your writing and ensuring grammatical accuracy. Practice using a variety of sentence structures and linking words to enhance coherence. Additionally, delve deeper into the implications of the data you present to demonstrate a higher level of analytical thinking.
🌸

🌸 Muslimbek task 224/03/2026, 04:56:09
There is a growing belief that certain offenders should be encouraged to do non-paid social work rather than being sent to the prison. This essay intends to partly agree with this view mainly because of failure to discourage crime and reducing prison population. One key reason why this claim is valid is that community service reduces prison overcrowding. Some criminals, a people who commited crime, who have commited a petty crime such as theft and vandalism do not need a strict punishment, and thats why they could be enforsed to do community work in order to reduce population of prison. Another argument supporting this idea is benefits for society. Minor prisoners could contribute through cleaning and helping elderly. Moreover, unpaid community work reduces government spending on prison. Therefore, convictors should do a social work. A major reason why this view is flawed is that this type of punishment can have a weak deterrent effect. If criminals know that they will only be required to do community service, they might not feel afraid of breaking the law. As a result, this approach may fail to discourage future crimes. Another factor that chalenges this view is that allowing offenders to remain in society can be risky. They may have more opportunities to commit further offences due to the lack of strict control. Thus, imprisonment is still necessary to ensure public safety and to act as a stronger form of punishment. To sum up, while there are some benefits of this claim such as reducing prison overcrowding, drawbacks like lack of deterrence should not be underesti
Word count: 263
AI Feedback
**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Clear Position**: The essay presents a clear position on the topic, indicating partial agreement with the idea of non-paid social work as an alternative to prison.
2. **Logical Structure**: The essay is organized into distinct paragraphs, each addressing a specific point related to the argument.
3. **Relevant Arguments**: The points made regarding prison overcrowding, societal benefits, and the potential risks of community service are relevant to the topic.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Language and Grammar Issues**: There are several grammatical errors and awkward phrases (e.g., "a people who commited crime," "enforsed," "convictors"). These detract from the clarity and professionalism of the writing.
2. **Incoherent Sentences**: Some sentences are poorly constructed, making it difficult to follow the argument (e.g., "this type of punishment can have a weak deterrent effect" could be more clearly articulated).
3. **Limited Development of Ideas**: While the essay presents some arguments, they are not fully developed. For instance, the societal benefits of community service could be elaborated upon with specific examples.
4. **Conclusion is Incomplete**: The conclusion is cut off and does not effectively summarize the main points or restate the writer's position clearly.

**Improvements:**
1. **Grammar and Vocabulary**: Focus on improving grammatical accuracy and expanding vocabulary. Consider using tools or resources for grammar checks.
2. **Sentence Structure**: Work on constructing clearer and more complex sentences. Ensure that each sentence flows logically to the next.
3. **Develop Arguments**: Provide more detailed examples and explanations for each argument. For instance, discuss specific types of community service and their benefits.
4. **Complete the Conclusion**: Ensure that the conclusion fully summarizes the essay's main points and reiterates the writer's stance clearly.

By addressing these weaknesses and focusing on the suggested improvements, the essay could achieve a higher band score in the IELTS Writing Task.

🌸 Ozodbek24/03/2026, 04:52:45
Information regarding the proportion of people from the ages of 23 to 65 in various occupations in one UK town called Ashby and in the UK as well is shown in the graph provided by 2008.  Overall, there are six different occupations where the percentage of personal service ranked first and the percentage of professional work comes last in the town. However, the whole UK experienced almost the same percentage.  Looking at the information in more detail, in Ashby, the fraction of personal service ranked first with 21%, followed by office work and construction work at 18% and 16%. Compared to the former occupations, the portion of shop workers and unemployed had the same percentage of 14%. Technical work and professional work had the lowest share at 9% and 8%.  In the case of the whole UK, the percentages are approximately the same. However, there are small changes in the positions.
Word count: 151
AI Feedback
**IELTS Writing Task Feedback**

**Band Score: 6.0**

**Strengths:**
1. **Overview:** The introduction provides a clear overview of the data presented in the graph, summarizing the main trends effectively.
2. **Organization:** The response is logically organized, with separate paragraphs for the overall trends and detailed analysis of Ashby and the UK.
3. **Data Presentation:** The writer includes specific percentages, which helps to substantiate the claims made about the different occupations.

**Weaknesses:**
1. **Task Achievement:** The response lacks a more comprehensive analysis of the data. While it mentions the percentages, it does not provide a thorough comparison between Ashby and the UK, nor does it explore the implications of these figures.
2. **Language Use:** There are instances of awkward phrasing and grammatical errors, such as "the fraction of personal service ranked first" (better phrased as "the percentage of people in personal service ranked first") and "the portion of shop workers and unemployed had the same percentage" (should be "the percentages of shop workers and unemployed were the same").
3. **Detail and Clarity:** The second paragraph could benefit from clearer comparisons and transitions between the different occupations. The use of "former occupations" could be confusing without clear reference to what those occupations are.

**Improvements:**
1. **Expand Analysis:** Provide a more in-depth comparison between Ashby and the UK, discussing any notable differences or similarities in the data. This could include mentioning specific occupations that are more or less prevalent in one area compared to the other.
2. **Enhance Language Accuracy:** Review grammatical structures and vocabulary for clarity and accuracy. Consider using synonyms or varied sentence structures to enhance the overall quality of the writing.
3. **Add Conclusions:** Conclude with a summary statement that encapsulates the main findings of the data, perhaps discussing what these trends might suggest about employment in Ashby compared to the broader UK context.

By addressing these areas, the response could achieve a higher band score in the IELTS Writing Task.

It is believed by some that seartain lawbreakers should be forced to perform unpaid works to help community instead of being send to prison. I partly agree with this view and this essay will explain the reasons for my opinion. On the one hand unpaid community work can be effective way to rehabilitaate minor offenders. This type of punishment allows them to take responsibility for their actions and develop a sense of discipline. For example,lowbreakers can help clean public areas or assist charitable organisations, which benefits socity. If wrongdoers found guilty by stealing food or anything else from the market, goverment could give them a chance to make a fresh start by being a sels assestant of caurse providing that they are under the supervision. By this convicts may become awere of social values as long as they interact with comunity in a positive way. This helps them to reintegrate back into society and help them to be low-abondining citizens. On the other hand, imprisonment is still essential for dangerous criminals even if coommunity service has some advantages. Voilent criminals should be kept in prison in order to protect society. But it does not mean that they should be toughon crime. Some rehabilitation actions can be provided, such as convirsations with psychotherapists because criminals may have serious mental health problems which are sometimes hard to recognise.Furthermore, the goverment must consider wether a criminal poses a threat to others before deciding on the type of punishment. Capital punishment can be used on condition that the offence is not serious, while authorities should take into consideration that some very serious crimes like murdering have to cary the full weight of the law. In conclution, although community servise is beneficial for minor crimes, prison remains necessary for serious offenders. I believe unpaid work should be used as an alternative punishment in suitable situations, as it can encourage personal improvement and benefit society.

It is commonly believed that prisoners should work for free for the community instead of putting them behind bars. In addition, it can be useful for both the community and the government. I partly agree with this trend.  On the one hand, working for free for the community might be beneficial for prisoners. Looking for the recently released prisoners, most of them regarded with bad behavior and responsibility. To reduce this number of issues, they have to work for the community. Even if put the prisoners behind the bars, it would not have an impact on their character unless they understand the hard work. Prisoners cleaning streets or helping in public services may learn discipline and social responsibility. On the other hand, there are many dangerous criminals. For instance, murder should not be given freedom. This type of prisoner poses a threat to society by bringing fear to the young generation, and, feasibly, some of them may teach a habit that should not be shown to the children. Furthermore, punishment as a form of working for the community may seem lenient. As a result, many people would not perceive it as a punishment, which can lead to difficulties in stopping the future of the crimes.  To conclude, the trend to prevent the prisoners by replacing the type of punishment may have positives, as long as prisoners are engaged in improving their behavior by helping the community for free and learning a new skill that can help them in their future career.
