🌸 Abdullokh09/12/2025, 01:54:35
In recent years, air quality has been deteriorating in many cities, and this has turned into a major issue. Although several reasons contribute to this growing problem, it is impact on ecosystems and human well-being can be extremely serious. One significant factor contributing to the issue is deforestation.When large areas of land are cleared for agriculture, construction, or industrial purposes, trees that normally absorb harmful gases are lost. As a result, pollutats remain in the atmosphere instead of being filtered out. Another major cause behind this problem is vehicle emmissions.Cars, buses, and trucks release large amounts of carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxides, and other harmful gases into the atmosphere. As the number of vehicles continues to grow in many cities, these pollutants acumulate and significantly reduce air quality. Heavy traffic and old, poorly maintained vehicles make the situation even worse, leading to dense smog and higher pollution levels. One major consequence of this trend is the significant damage it causes to the environment. Polluted air contains harmful gases, such as sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides, which can mix with rainwater to form acid rain. This gradually weakens trees, and contaminates rivers and lakes, harming plants, fish, and other wildlife. Over time these effects disrrupt ecosystems, reduce biodiversity, and make natural habitats less resilient to challenges like climate change. Another major consequence of this trend is its impact on public health. Breathing polluted air expozes people to harmful gases and particles, which can cause respiratory problems such as asthma and bronchitis. Long-term exposure may also increase the risk of heart disease and weaken the immune system. Children and the elderly are particularly vulnerable and overall, poor air quality can reduce life quality and place a burden on healthcare systems. In conclusion, the rise in air pollution is caused by several factors, including vehicle emissions, industrial activities, and deforestation. This problem has serious consequences, both for the environment, through issues like acid rain and ecosystem damage, and for human health, through respiratory and other diseases.

Rubric: In many urban areas, the rise in air pollution is becoming a significant concern. What are the main causes of this increase, and what effects does it have on environment and public health?


Essay:

In recent decades, air contamination is becoming more and more considerable issue. There are several reasons which can be put forward to explain this, and there are significant impacts on natural world and public health.


One major factor contributing to this concern is increased use of

vehicles. More cars and buses on the roads lead to higher level of harmful gases. Many vehicles are emitting exhaust fumes such as nitrogen oxide and carbon dioxide. These gases often have detrimental impacts on environment.

Another major cause behind this problem is dependence on fossil fuels. Many cities rely heavily on fossil fuels such as coal and oil, as the main source of energy to run industries and produce electricity. When they are burned, they release large amount of toxic gases. Since urban areas need a lot of energy, they continue to burn fossil fuels contaminating the air.


One major consequence

of this trend is climate change. Climate change is mainly caused by rising greenhouse gases in the atmosphere. These gases trap the heat from the sun, causing the Earth to warn up. As a result, cities are experiencing hotter temperatures.

Another essential impact of this issue is respiratory problems. Pollutants such as dust and hazardous gases are irritating people’s lungs and airways making it more challenging to breathe. Long-term exposure can cause chronic diseases such as lung cancer and asthma.

To sum up, there are several reasons why air contamination is devastative

issue, and this tendency can result in a number of negative impacts on nature and individual life’s causing respiratory problems.

There is a growing belief that authorities should ban all types of advertisement. This essay argues that this view is misguided because advantages of advertisement are more significant. Because companies pay to display ads, viewers can enjoy entertainment and information for free. Thus, advertisement should not be banned due to these arguments. To sum up, I am convinced that this view is flawed because of free content and useful information advertisements provide. If used in moderation and with a clear purpose, ads would be not only useful but also a good marketing way of introducing the product.

Some people hold the view that all types of advertising is completely unnecessary and should be blanket ban. This information functions is helpful for enabling people to compare similar products and decide which option best fits their needs and budget. If all advertising were banned, buyers would have far less opportunity to clear and organized product information, making the purchasing process more confusing. In conclusion, I strongly believe that advertising should not be completely banned, as it helps consumers make informed choices and plays a vital role in supporting business growth. While some adverts may be misleading, appropriate regulation is a better solution than a total ban.

🌸 Sevinch06/12/2025, 04:21:43
Rubric:some people think there should be a complete ban in all forms of advertising to what extent do you agree or disagree Essay In recent years, the influence of advertising has become so widespread that some individuals argue for a blanket ban on all forms of promotional activity. While I acknowledge that certain advertisements can be misleading or harmful, I completely disagree with the idea of a total prohibition. Instead of removing advertising altogether, it would be more sensible to restrict the most problematic types and encourage ethical marketing practices. To begin with, advertising plays a crucial role in supporting the economy, especially for SMEs that rely heavily on promotional tools to reach potential customers. For many small companies, completely imposing a ban on advertising would limit their ability to compete with larger corporations and could eventually push them into bankruptcy. Without the visibility that promotions offer, these enterprises would struggle to introduce new products, raise awareness, or build customer trust. Moreover, modern advertising has become part of mainstream culture, shaping consumer habits and informing people about available goods and services, from household appliances to educational opportunities. Another key reason why a full ban would be unrealistic is that advertising helps consumers make informed choices. Rather than eliminating it, governments could reallocate resources towards stricter monitoring and enforcing guidelines against deceptive content. This approach would ensure that campaigns remain honest and socially responsible. For example, unhealthy or manipulative advertisements could be limited to specific platforms or shown at restricted hours, reducing potential harm without implementing a total ban. In conclusion, although concerns about the negative effects of advertising are understandable, enforcing a blanket ban would do more harm than good. It is far more reasonable to restrict misleading or harmful promotions while encouraging transparent and ethical campaigns that benefit both businesses and consumers.

🌸 ozodbek06/12/2025, 04:16:48
Advertising has become a part of daily life, and some people argue that it should be completely banned. Although advertisements can sometimes mislead people, I believe that banning all advertising would create more problems than it solves. One reason why some individuals support a full ban is that advertisements can be manipulative. Companies often exaggerate the quality of their products to attract more customers. As a result, people may waste money on items that they do not really need. Another concern is that advertising affects young children. For example, many ads show toys, sweets or fast food, which can influence children’s behaviour and even harm their health in the long term. From this point of view, banning advertising seems reasonable. However, despite these disadvantages, a complete ban is unrealistic and unnecessary. Advertising plays an important role in the modern economy. It helps businesses introduce new products and services, and without it, many small companies would struggle to survive. Moreover, advertisements provide useful information for consumers. For example, people learn about discounts, new technologies, or educational courses, which can be beneficial for their daily lives. Instead of banning all ads, governments can set strict rules to prevent false or harmful advertising. This is a more balanced and practical solution. In conclusion, although advertisements sometimes have negative effects, I do not support a total ban. In my opinion, advertising is necessary for economic development and consumer choice, but it should be controlled to ensure that it remains honest and safe.

🌸 Glazkov Vladimir06/12/2025, 03:11:30
Some people think that there should be a complete ban on all forms of advertising. To what extent do you agree or disagree? Many people believe that advertising should be completely prohibited because it has a negative influence on society. Although advertising can sometimes be misleading, I disagree with the idea of removing it fully. On the one hand, some individuals argue that advertising encourages people to buy things they do not really need. For example, companies often exaggerate the benefits of their products, which can create false expectations and lead to disappointment. In addition, children are easily influenced by bright and attractive commercials, which may push them to demand unhealthy food or unnecessary toys. These effects make some people feel that a ban on advertising would protect consumers. However, despite these drawbacks, advertising also has important advantages that cannot be ignored. Firstly, it helps businesses promote their products and reach customers. This is especially important for small companies that depend on advertising to survive in a competitive market. Secondly, advertisements provide useful information about new products, services, and discounts, allowing consumers to make smarter choices. Finally, advertising supports many industries, such as television, online platforms, and sports organisations, by providing essential funding. In my view, rather than banning advertising, governments should introduce clear rules. They could limit advertisements aimed at children, control false claims, and ensure that companies provide accurate information. This approach would reduce the negative impacts while keeping the benefits. In conclusion, although advertising can sometimes be harmful, a complete ban is not a practical solution. With better regulation, advertising can remain an important and helpful part for modern society.

Rubric: Some people think that there should be a complete ban on all forms of advertising. To extent do you agree or disagree? Essay: There are several reasons which can be put forward to explain why advertising should not be banned, and there are signaficant impacts on both the economy and comsumers .Although some people trigger harmful behaviours, i disagree that a total ban is the best solution. One signaficant factor cantributing to this issue is the crucial economic function of advertising. Advertising play a major part in helping businesses attract custumors, and banning it would lead to serious financial difficulties, especially for small companies. Economic growth is largely generated by commercial activity, which in turn results from effective promotion. Another major couse behind my disagreement is that advertising creates countless job opportunities. Another signaficant impact of advertising is its positive effect on consumers. Advertising contributes to raising public awareness of new products, services, and innovations. This often results in people making more informed purchasing decisions, Moreover, public health and safety campains - such as those encouraging recycling or reducing smoking - are the result of goverenment advertising. If advertisement were removed, this could have serious implication for public education and community well being. While it is true that misleading advertisement can trigger unethical behavior or confusion, this problem can mainly be attributed to weak regulation rather than advertising itself. As a result, stricter monitoring would be far more effective than a universal ban. A more balanced approach is to regulate harmful advertising rather than eliminate all of promotion.
Word count: 301
Teacher Feedback
🔹 Task Response — Band 7.0–7.5

Strengths:

You clearly disagree with a complete ban and maintain this position throughout.

Your ideas are relevant, well-developed, and logical.

You support your opinion with clear reasons:

economic importance

job creation

consumer awareness

public health campaigns

You address the opposing view (harmful advertising) and refute it effectively by suggesting regulation instead of a ban.

What prevents a higher score:

The introduction could be slightly clearer and more concise.

A few ideas could be illustrated with specific examples to strengthen arguments.

✅ Estimated band: 7.0–7.5

🔹 Coherence & Cohesion — Band 7.5

What you did well:

Clear paragraph structure: introduction, two body paragraphs, counterargument, conclusion.

Logical progression of ideas.

Effective use of linking devices:

Although, Moreover, As a result, While it is true that

Minor issues:

A few punctuation errors interrupt flow.

One or two sentences are slightly long and could be split.

Example:

❌ Designers, marketers, and media specialists are rely on this industry…
✅ Designers, marketers, and media specialists rely on this industry…

✅ Estimated band: 7.5

🔹 Lexical Resource (Vocabulary) — Band 6.5–7.0

Strong points:

Good range of academic vocabulary:

significant, contribute, economic growth, regulation, unethical behaviour

Appropriate tone for an opinion essay.

Major issue: spelling
There are many spelling errors, which IELTS penalises heavily:

Incorrect Correct
signaficant significant
comsumers consumers
custumors customers
cantributing contributing
couse cause
campains campaigns
goverenment government
siome some
contunue continue
advertisement advertising (in general use)

These reduce your lexical score despite good word choice.

✅ Estimated band: 6.5–7.0

🔹 Grammatical Range & Accuracy — Band 6.5

Strengths:

Good variety of sentence structures.

Effective use of complex sentences (Although, While it is true that…).

Errors do not usually block meaning.

Common grammar problems:

Subject–verb agreement:

❌ Advertising play a major part

✅ Advertising plays a major part

Verb form errors:

❌ are rely on

✅ rely on

Article misuse:

❌ therefore complete ban

✅ therefore, a complete ban

These errors are noticeable but manageable.

✅ Estimated band: 6.5

🟢 Overall Estimated IELTS Writing Task 2 Band Score:
⭐ Band 7.0

This is a strong Band 7 essay with:

Clear position

Logical argumentation

Good coherence

Appropriate academic tone

However, frequent spelling mistakes and minor grammar issues prevent it from reaching Band 7.5–8.

🔹 What to Fix to Reach Band 7.5–8

Proofread spelling carefully (this is your biggest weakness)

Fix subject–verb agreement errors

Use 1–2 specific examples

Slightly tighten the introduction

In recent years, there has been a growing debate about whether all forms of advertising should be completely banned. Some people argue that advertisements have too many negative effects on society and should therefore be removed entirely. However, I strongly disagree with this idea. Firstly, I disagree with a complete ban because advertising is essential for the growth of businesses. It allows companies, especially small and newly established ones, to introducce their products to potential customers. Without advertisements, many busineses would struggle to reach a wide audience and compete in the market. As a result, this could slow down economic development and lead to fewer job opportunities for people. Secondly, advertisements play an important role in providing people with valuable information that they might not discover on their own. For example, many individuals learn about new products, technological improvemnts, or special discounts through advertisements. This helps customers make more informed choices and compare different options before buying something. In addition, advertisements often inform people about essential services such as medical check-ups, educational courses, or job openings. If all forms of advertising were banned, the public would have limited access to these updates, and this could make everyday life less convenient. Instead of introducing a complete ban, governments should regulate advertsing to ensure that information remains accurate and does not mislead consumers.
In conclusion, although some people believe that advertising should be completely prohibited, I think such a ban would be impractical and unnecessary. Advertisingg not only helps businesses grow and supports the economy

Organized trips to remote countries are increasingly common. Is it positive or negative development for individuals and the environment? Organized travel to distant countries are becoming popular nowadays. While many people enjoy such opportunities to explore distant countries, there are several reasons which can be put forward to explain this and there are significant impact on people and nature. One major consequence of this trend is travel distant locations can broaden your horizons. Explore different cultures and learn about their traditions can help people develop new skills. By observing other countries live, travelers may learn new ways of cooking , building or communicating. Experiencing different traditions can also inspire people to become more creative. Travel enhance people growth with many ways. Visiting remote destinations adjust people to new lifestyle and environment faster. Such experiences can build greater confidence , flexibility and the abilaty to handle unfamiliar situations. Another significant impact of this issue is visiting to far countries can harm the environment. Many natural areas are fragile and tourists can cause pollution, damage the nature and disturb wildlife. For instance forests, mountains and cliffs may suffer if many tourists visited. It shows that people enjoy traveling but it can negatively effect on natural word. However the negative effects can be reduced with tourism. Tours should follow eco- friendly rules and support wild life protection to maintain the nature. If they focus on that carefully trips can benefit both people and environment. In conclusion, there are several reasons why more travelers are choosing travel to far away distinctions and this tendency can result in a number of positive impacts on both tourists and nature. Travel have many negative sides on environment but it can give exciting experiences and cultural knowledge.
Word count: 289
Teacher Feedback
📌 1. Task Response – Band 6

Strengths:
• You discuss both impacts (positive & negative).
• You provide examples and explanation.

Problems:
• Introduction has unclear meaning.
• You do not explicitly decide whether the trend is positive or negative.
• Some ideas are repeated or loosely connected.

⸻

📌 2. Coherence & Cohesion – Band 5

Strengths:
• Paragraphs exist.
• Logical progression.

Problems:
• Weak topic sentences.
• Several sentences are fragments, not complete sentences:
❌ “Explore different cultures and learn about their traditions…”
• Linking words sometimes missing.
• Repetitive structure.

⸻

📌 3. Lexical Resource – Band 5

Strengths:
• Topic vocabulary is used (wildlife, culture, traditions).

Problems:

Many unnatural or incorrect collocations:

❌ “travel distant locations can broaden your horizons”
❌ “observe other countries live”
❌ “enhance people growth with many ways”
❌ “effect on natural word”
❌ “travel to far away distinctions” → (wrong word; “destinations”)

These errors limit the score.

⸻

📌 4. Grammar Range & Accuracy – Band 4.5–5

Frequent errors:
• subject–verb agreement
• article misuse
• wrong verb forms
• sentence fragments
• punctuation problems
• incorrect plural forms
• run-on sentences

Examples:

❌ “Organized travel to distant countries are becoming popular…”
(“travel” = singular → is becoming)

❌ “Travel have many negative sides…”
(should be Travel has many negative effects)

⸻

⭐ FINAL BAND: 5.0–5.5

You need clearer grammar, accurate collocations, and more precise structure to reach Band 6.5–7.

Band 7 Model Answer

Organised travel to distant countries has become increasingly popular in recent years. Many people enjoy exploring remote destinations, and although this trend brings some benefits, it also has important consequences for both people and the natural environment.

One major positive impact of travelling to distant locations is that it can broaden a person’s horizons. Experiencing different cultures and traditions allows travellers to develop new skills and learn new ways of living. For example, visitors may discover new cooking methods, building styles or communication habits by observing how people in other countries live. Such experiences can also inspire creativity and encourage personal growth. In addition, travelling to remote destinations helps individuals adapt more quickly to unfamiliar environments, building confidence, flexibility and the ability to handle new situations.

However, visiting faraway regions can also cause harm to the environment. Many natural areas are extremely fragile, and large numbers of tourists may damage wildlife habitats, disturb animals or contribute to pollution. Forests, mountains and coastal cliffs, for instance, may suffer when too many visitors arrive at the same time. Although these impacts are serious, they can be reduced through responsible tourism. Guided tours that follow eco-friendly rules and support wildlife protection can help preserve nature while still allowing people to explore remote destinations.

In conclusion, there are several reasons why more travellers are choosing to visit faraway places, and this trend has both positive and negative effects. While travel can damage the environment, it also provides exciting experiences and valuable cultural knowledge for individuals.
AI Feedback
🌸
💗
🌸 Farida04/12/2025, 04:37:14
A growing number of people now choose to join guided trips to distant areas. This essay will examine the positive and negative aspects of this development. I believe this trend largely positive for humans and natural world. On the one hand, there are several negative aspects of guided tours.First and one of the major reasons is some remote areas are extremely sensitive for large groups. Many of these areas have ecosystems which develop with very little human contact, so even small disbalances can lead to long-term damage. Second issue is that remote places often lack medical facilities, transport, or clean water.This means that it could be really challenging to find travel methods with minimal risk or even safe water supply. On the other hand, these trips can offer several important benefits for individuals and the environment. For the reason that commercially organized travel to distant destinations helps people broader their worldview and cultural awareness. This trips to remote regions helps to learn unique traditions,lifestyles and language for travelers. As a result,they could become open-minded and tolerant,as exploring unfamiliar cultures helps them challenge stereotypes and appreciate different ways of life.The main environmental advantage is that it raises global awareness of environment issues. Tourists learn about endangered species or fragile ecosystems and may support conservation campaigns. In conclusion, although guided trips to remote regions can pose risks to fragile ecosystems and may lack essential infrastructure, they also provide meaningful cultural experiences and promote environmental awareness. On balance, I believe this trend is largely positive, as responsible tourism can help protect natural areas while allowing individuals to develop broader perspectives.
Word count: 267
Teacher Feedback
📌 1. Task Response – 7

Strengths:
• Addresses both positives and negatives.
• Clear overall opinion.
• Examples and explanations provided.

Issues:
• The opinion in the introduction is unclear and grammatically incorrect:
❌ “I believe this trend largely positive for humans and natural world.”
Needs: “I believe this trend is largely positive for both people and the natural world.”
• Some points need stronger development.

⸻

📌 2. Coherence & Cohesion – 6

Strengths:
• Clear paragraphing
• Logical overall structure

Issues:
• Problems with paragraph openings:
❌ “On the one hand, there are several negative aspects of guided tours.First and one of the major reasons…”
→ Missing space, unnatural phrasing, grammar issues.
• Some sentences lack cohesion or smooth linking.

⸻

📌 3. Lexical Resource – 6

Strengths:
• Good topic vocabulary: sensitive areas, fragile ecosystems, conservation campaigns.

Issues:
• Many incorrect or unnatural collocations:
❌ “some remote areas are extremely sensitive for large groups” → “sensitive to large numbers of visitors”
❌ “small disbalances” → “small imbalances”
❌ “helps people broader their worldview” → “broaden”
❌ “For the reason that commercially organized travel…” (awkward)
• Repetition of simple words (“this trips”)
• Occasional vocabulary misuse.

⸻

📌 4. Grammar Range & Accuracy – 5.5

Strengths:
• Some complex sentences
• Attempts at more advanced grammar

Issues:

Frequent grammar mistakes:
• Missing articles: “this trips”, “natural world” → “the natural world”
• Wrong verb forms: “helps people broader”
• Wrong sentence structure
• Missing commas
• Some unclear sentences:
❌ “Second issue is that remote places often lack medical facilities, transport, or clean water.This means that it could be really challenging to find travel methods with minimal risk or even safe water supply.”

These errors reduce your score.

⸻

⭐ FINAL BAND: 6.0–6.5

With grammar and vocabulary corrections, this could reach Band 7+.
Band 7 Model Answer

A growing number of people now choose to join guided trips to distant regions. This essay will examine the positive and negative aspects of this development. Overall, I believe this trend is largely beneficial for both individuals and the natural environment.

On the one hand, organised tours to remote areas can create several problems. Many of these locations are extremely sensitive to large numbers of visitors. Their ecosystems have developed with minimal human contact, meaning even small disturbances may lead to long-term ecological damage. Another issue is the lack of infrastructure. Remote places often have limited medical facilities, weak transportation networks and unreliable access to clean water. As a result, travellers may face difficulties in emergencies or be exposed to unsafe conditions.

On the other hand, these trips also provide important benefits for both people and the environment. Commercially organised travel allows individuals to broaden their worldview and develop cultural awareness. Visiting remote regions exposes travellers to unique traditions, lifestyles and languages, helping them become more open-minded and tolerant. In addition, guided tours can promote environmental awareness. Tourists often learn about endangered species or fragile ecosystems and may later support conservation initiatives after witnessing these issues first-hand.

In conclusion, although guided trips to remote areas can put pressure on fragile environments and present safety challenges due to limited infrastructure, they also offer valuable cultural experiences and raise awareness of environmental protection. On balance, I believe this trend is largely positive, especially when tourism is managed responsibly.

Band-7 Sample Answer

Nowadays, it has become increasingly common for people to travel to foreign countries. This trend is driven by several factors, and although it brings certain benefits for travellers, it also causes significant environmental damage.

One positive impact of international travel is that it allows young people to broaden their interests and learn about other cultures. By visiting different countries, travellers can experience new cuisines, traditional clothing and unique customs that they would never encounter at home. These cultural exchanges help young people develop open-mindedness and encourage tourism growth, as countries share their traditions with a wider audience.

However, the negative effects of this trend are more serious, particularly for the environment and, in some cases, for individuals. Long-distance flights produce large amounts of carbon emissions, which contribute to climate change. Road trips also rely heavily on petrol and diesel vehicles, causing air pollution that harms wildlife and ecosystems. In addition, travelling to distant countries may pose health risks for certain passengers, such as stress or high blood pressure during long flights. These issues show that while travel can be enjoyable, it can also create environmental damage and safety concerns.

In conclusion, although travelling to other countries offers cultural and personal benefits, it also results in significant environmental problems. In my view, the advantages should be enjoyed responsibly, and travellers must be aware of the negative effects associated with global tourism.

🌸

🌸 Turapova Sevinch04/12/2025, 04:22:40
Rubric: Organized trips to remote countries are increasingly common. Is it a positive or negative development for individuals and the environment.

Many people now travel to distant and untouched regions, and this trend is becoming more widespread. While such trips offer personal benefits, I believe they bring more harm than good both for individuals and the environment.
On the one hand, organised trips to remote areas can be beneficial for travellers. Visiting isolated locations allows people to experience landscapes and cultures that they would never encounter in cities or popular tourist destinations. This can broaden their horizons and help them appreciate nature on a deeper level. In addition, remote travel often involves physical activities such as hiking or climbing, which may contribute to better health and a sense of achievement. For some individuals, escaping busy urban life and spending time in quiet, untouched places can also reduce stress and improve emotional well-being. On the other hand, I believe this trend has more negative consequences, especially for the environment. Many remote areas contain fragile ecosystems that can be easily damaged by increased human presence. For example, visitors may unintentionally disturb wildlife, leave waste behind or contribute to soil erosion. Moreover, organised trips usually require long flights or off-road vehicles, both of which increase carbon emissions and accelerate environmental degradation. From an individual perspective, travelling to such isolated regions can also be risky. Harsh weather conditions, limited medical support and unfamiliar surroundings may put travellers in dangerous situations. In conclusion, although travelling to remote places can offer unique experiences and personal growth, I think the negative impacts on the environment and the potential risks for individuals make this trend largely harmful.
Word count: 278

🌸 Positive or negative development AKRAMJONOV MUSLIMBEK04/12/2025, 04:18:04
Rubric: Organized trips to remote countries are increasingly common. Is it a positive or negative development for individuals and the environment? Essay: Thanks to the world being more connected, there are now opportunities for people to travel overseas. While the proposal has some potential drawbacks, I believe the benefits carry more weight overall. On the one hand, it is undeniable that the most significant drawback of travelling abroad is high carbon footprint. Approximately 9 percent of global CO2 emissions are caused by traveling. Most of these emissions are produced when tourists fly or drive long distances, so the longer the journey, the higher levels of carbon dioxide that are emitted. Moreover, tourists are involved in damaging ancient artifacts. Many countries have their own historical landmarks, and tourists could accidentally break some medieval dishes or paintings. For instance, in Machu Picchu in Peru, one tourist accidentally scratched stones and walked into restricted area while taking photos. As a result, government limited daily number of visitors and put more security to protect it. Therefore, travelling abroad has some negative aspects. On the other hand, I would argue that these disadvantages are outweighed by positive aspects. One of the biggest advantages of travelling abroad is improving language skills. People, who took a trip to foreign country, could learn local language, for example by watching local television or chatting with natives. Furthermore, when tourism increases, the government invests more. If more and more individuals will travel to certain area, authorities will be encouraged to invest more in this area to attract tourists making it easier and safer to travel. For instance, building tourist centre and better roads. Therefore, I believe that mass tourism has benefits not only for tourists, but also for locals. To sum up, despite some negative aspects, I believe that the benefits of mass tourism are more significant for the reasons aforementioned

BAND 7:

Organised trips to remote countries are becoming increasingly common thanks to improved global connectivity. Although this trend has some negative consequences, I believe the benefits for individuals and local communities are more significant overall.

On the one hand, travelling to distant destinations can create environmental and cultural problems. The most serious issue is the high carbon footprint associated with long-distance travel. It is estimated that around 9% of global CO₂ emissions come from tourism, particularly from long-haul flights and road transport. Another drawback is the potential damage to historical or cultural sites. Some tourists behave carelessly around ancient landmarks, which may lead to accidental harm. For example, in Machu Picchu, a visitor once scratched stones while attempting to take photos in a restricted area. As a result, the government introduced stricter entry limits to protect the site. These issues show that tourism can have harmful effects if not managed responsibly.

On the other hand, I believe these disadvantages are outweighed by the benefits. Travelling abroad allows people to improve their language abilities and cultural understanding by communicating with locals and experiencing everyday life firsthand. Furthermore, when a destination attracts more tourists, local authorities often invest in better infrastructure, such as roads, visitor centres and public transport. This not only makes travel safer and more comfortable but also creates jobs and stimulates economic development in remote communities. For these reasons, organised trips can be advantageous for both travellers and local residents.

In conclusion, although such trips may contribute to environmental damage, the personal and economic benefits are more significant. With proper management, organised tourism can bring positive outcomes for individuals and the wider community.

Band 7 Model Answer

Organised trips to remote countries are becoming increasingly popular. While this trend offers some positive experiences, I believe the negative consequences for the environment and local communities are more significant.

On the one hand, travelling to remote places can provide several benefits for individuals. Such trips allow travellers to explore unique landscapes, observe rare wildlife and learn about cultures that may be very different from their own. These experiences can broaden their horizons, increase cultural awareness and contribute to personal growth. In addition, organised tours often provide opportunities to build confidence. Navigating unfamiliar environments, languages and customs helps travellers develop independence and self-reliance, which are valuable life skills.

However, despite these advantages, I would argue that the drawbacks are more substantial. The biggest concern is the environmental impact. Many remote regions have fragile ecosystems that can be easily damaged by large numbers of tourists. Activities such as hiking, camping or off-road driving may disturb wildlife habitats and cause pollution. Furthermore, increased tourism can affect the local economy in negative ways. A sudden rise in visitors may lead to higher living costs, creating pressure on indigenous communities. Tourism can also influence cultural identity, as local people may feel compelled to change their traditions or adopt foreign habits to attract visitors.

In conclusion, although organised trips to remote countries offer memorable experiences and personal development opportunities, these benefits are outweighed by the environmental damage and cultural disruption they may cause. Therefore, I believe this trend is more negative than positive.

Band 7 Model Answer

Organised trips to remote countries are becoming increasingly common in today’s world. This trend has raised discussions about whether it is beneficial or harmful. Although there are some drawbacks, I believe it is mostly a positive development.

One negative effect of this trend is the damage it can cause to the environment. Increased tourism often results in pollution, waste, and disturbances to wildlife. Remote areas, which are usually fragile, may suffer from erosion or habitat destruction when large numbers of visitors arrive. Long-distance flights also generate significant carbon emissions, contributing to climate change. As a result, some people argue that organised trips harm natural ecosystems.

However, there are several strong reasons why this trend can be considered positive for individuals and local communities. Travelling to remote places allows people to experience unique cultures and breathtaking natural landscapes, which can have a profound impact on personal growth. Organised tours also make these journeys safer, as guides provide support and reliable information. Another advantage is that tourism contributes to the local economy by creating jobs and generating income for small communities. Therefore, when managed responsibly, such trips can bring significant benefits.

In conclusion, although organised trips to remote countries may lead to environmental challenges, they also offer valuable personal and economic advantages. Overall, I believe the benefits of this development outweigh the drawbacks.

🌸 Владимир Глазков03/12/2025, 06:15:15
*Organized trips to remote countries are increasingly common. Is it a positive or negative development for individuals and the environment?* In recent years, travelling to remote destinations through organised tours has become increasingly popular. Although this trend brings certain drawbacks for the environment, I believe its overall impact on individuals and local communities is largely positive. On the one hand, organised trips to isolated regions can provide significant benefits for individuals. Firstly, such tours offer a safe way for people to explore unfamiliar places that would be difficult to visit independently. Tour companies usually provide experienced guides, transportation and logistical support, which allows travellers to focus on enjoying the culture and nature without worrying about safety. Secondly, these trips often promote cultural understanding. When tourists interact with indigenous communities, they gain deeper knowledge about traditional lifestyles, which can broaden their worldview and foster respect for different cultures. On the other hand, the growing popularity of organised travel to remote areas can have negative consequences for the environment. Large groups of tourists may put pressure on fragile ecosystems. For example, increased foot traffic in rainforests or mountain regions can damage vegetation, disturb wildlife and contribute to soil erosion. Moreover, tour operators sometimes prioritise profit over sustainability, which can lead to waste, pollution and overuse of natural resources. If not managed properly, this form of tourism risks harming the very destinations people come to admire. In conclusion, while organised tourism in remote areas may cause environmental challenges, I consider it a mostly positive development due to the cultural and educational benefits it offers to travellers. With responsible management, the environmental impact can be significantly reduced.

Band 7.5 Model Answer

Thanks to increased global connectivity, people now have more opportunities to travel to distant and remote destinations. While this trend offers some benefits, I believe that the drawbacks are more significant for both individuals and the environment.

On the one hand, there are several positive aspects of organized trips to remote countries. These journeys allow travellers to experience unique cultures, landscapes, and traditions that they would never encounter in their everyday lives. Such exposure can broaden their perspectives and deepen their appreciation of global diversity. Moreover, organized tourism can generate income for local communities. This financial support often encourages governments and residents to protect natural areas and preserve wildlife, since these attractions draw more visitors.

On the other hand, travelling to remote locations also presents serious disadvantages. Many of these regions have fragile ecosystems that can be easily damaged by large numbers of tourists. Increased foot traffic, waste, and transportation can disturb wildlife and pollute land and water. In addition, organized trips to isolated communities may disrupt traditional lifestyles, leading to cultural commercialisation or a loss of privacy for local residents. Finally, long-distance travel typically requires air transport, which significantly increases carbon emissions and contributes to global environmental problems.

In conclusion, although organized trips to remote countries can offer cultural benefits and economic support, the negative impacts on the environment and local communities are more substantial. Therefore, I believe that the drawbacks outweigh the advantages.

Many believe that young people should spend more of their free time with their family instead of other entertainment. It is commonly believed that young people should spend more of their leisure time with their family members rather than devote time to entertainment. This essay argues that this view is misguided because balanced lifestyle is healthier than spending all time with family and various forms of activities which reduce stress. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that it ignores the importance of teenager's social and emotional development. Relying too much on parents can limit their ability to adapt to different environments. If a young child spends almost all his time with parents or siblings, they may become overly reliant on family support. It could be really challenging for them to communicate with strangers or even their classmates. Spending time with their peers helps them build strong confidence in situations outside their home. Another key point is that most children experience a lot of stress and the best way to reduce it is entertainment. Because young individuals often face pressure from school, exams, and high expectations from parents and society.And because of this,entertainment plays an important role in helping them maintain mental and emotional balance.Teenagers often have a really busy workload, a lot of homework and exams so entertainment activities such as sport really helps students relax and learn how to work with team. In conclusion, I firmly disagree with the idea that children should spend most of their leisure time with their family members. Due to the importance of independence in real-life situations and emotional and mental state with entertainment and group activities provide.

Band 7 Model Answer

Many believe that young people should spend most of their free time with their families instead of engaging in other forms of entertainment. However, this essay argues that this view is misguided because young people need a balanced lifestyle that supports both independence and emotional well-being.

A major reason why this belief is unconvincing is that it overlooks the importance of teenagers’ social and emotional development. Spending nearly all their time with their families can make young people overly dependent on their parents and unable to adapt to different social environments. For example, children who rarely interact with peers may struggle to communicate confidently with classmates or strangers. In contrast, spending time with friends helps them develop independence, build confidence, and learn how to handle real-life situations outside the home.

Another key point is that entertainment and social activities are essential for reducing stress. Young people today face considerable pressure from school, exams, and high expectations from both parents and society. Recreational activities such as sports, clubs, or creative hobbies allow them to relax and maintain a healthy emotional balance. For instance, participating in team sports not only helps teenagers unwind but also teaches teamwork and cooperation—skills that cannot be gained through family interactions alone.

In conclusion, I firmly disagree with the idea that young people should spend most of their leisure time with their families. Although family relationships are important, entertainment and social activities play an essential role in building independence, reducing stress, and supporting healthy emotional development.

Band 7 Model Answer

Spending time with family is often considered essential for a child’s emotional and social development. While some people argue that children should focus more on friends and other activities, I strongly believe that family time is far more beneficial.

To begin with, spending time with family helps children build strong emotional connections. When young people communicate regularly with their parents, they feel supported, understood, and more secure. This emotional foundation increases their confidence and helps them deal with problems in a healthy way. For instance, teenagers who discuss their challenges at home often make better decisions because they receive guidance based on real experience. Moreover, families play a crucial role in teaching important values such as respect, responsibility, and honesty. These qualities are rarely learned from formal lessons; instead, children absorb them through everyday interactions at home.

However, it is true that children also need time with friends and at school. Socialising helps them develop teamwork, independence, and communication skills. In my view, this does not replace the unique support that families provide. A healthy balance is ideal, but family time should remain the priority because it lays the foundation for a child’s character and future behaviour.

In conclusion, although spending time with friends is beneficial, I believe that family time is far more important for a child’s emotional growth and long-term development.

There is a growing belief that adolescence ought to allocate more of their past times with their families. I accept that young people should prioritise family time, as it provides support and helps shape their character. To begin with, spending free time with family creates a stable emotional foundation that adolescents often need during this sensitive stage of life. Young people commonly deal with academic pressure, changes in friendships and uncertainty about their future. When they regularly interact with parents or siblings they can share their worries and receive advice from those who understand them best. For instance, simple activities such as cooking together or having a walk in the evening allows to teenagers to open up and feel less isolated. This emotional closeness not only reduces stress but also teaches them how to build healthy relationships outside the house. In addition, family time plays a crucial role in developing important values and life skills that entertainment alone cannot offer. While hobbies, games or social media provides relaxation, they rarely teach responsibility, empathy or patience. In contrast, participating in family routines such as helping with choreses or taking part in cultural traditions, encourages youngsters to become more considerate and disciplined. These experiences prepare them for adulthood by showing them how to cooperat respect others and manage real-life responsibilities. As a result, young people who spend sufficient time with their families often grow into more confident and well-rounded individuals. In conclusion, although entertainment has its place in a young person’s life, I believe that family time is far more valuable. It strengthens emotional wellbeing and helps adolescents develop the essential skills needed for their future.

Band 7.5 Model Answer

There is a growing belief that adolescents should spend more of their free time with their families. I agree with this view, as family time provides emotional support and plays an important role in shaping young people’s character.

To begin with, spending time with family creates a stable emotional foundation that adolescents often need during this sensitive stage of life. Young people frequently experience academic pressure, changes in friendships, and uncertainty about their future. When they regularly interact with parents or siblings, they can share their concerns and receive guidance from those who understand them best. For instance, simple activities such as cooking together or taking evening walks allow teenagers to open up and feel less isolated. This emotional closeness not only reduces stress but also teaches them how to build healthy relationships outside the home.

In addition, family time helps develop important values and life skills that entertainment alone cannot provide. While hobbies, games, or social media offer relaxation, they rarely teach responsibility, empathy, or patience. In contrast, participating in family routines—such as helping with chores or taking part in cultural traditions—encourages young people to become more disciplined and considerate. These experiences prepare them for adulthood by teaching them how to cooperate, respect others, and manage real-life responsibilities. As a result, adolescents who spend sufficient time with their families often grow into more confident and well-rounded individuals.

In conclusion, although entertainment is an enjoyable part of a young person’s life, I believe that family time is far more valuable. It strengthens emotional well-being and helps adolescents develop essential skills for their future.

Many believe that young people should spend more of their free time with their family instead of other entertainment. Do you agree or disagree? It is widely believed that the young people ought to spend a more of their spare time at home with family rather than enjoying other leisure activities. I completely disagree with this view because independence, confidence, and communication skills should be encouraged in young people. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that spending leisure time at home with family does not always lead to strong relationships. Most young people already meet their family members every day at home and they usually talk to their parents about school, personal things and any other things. Forcing them to stay at home can make them feel controller or independent. Young people often have different interests from their parents so using time with them might feel boring and uncomfortable. Family time can feel repetitive and boring rather than build meaningful relationships. Another factor that challanges this claim is that entertainment and leisure activities outside the home provide opportunities to grow. Participate in clubs, volunteer works or use time with peers allows them to develop teamwork, leadership communication skills. Moreover , exploring new activities and environments helps youn people discover their talents which can be useful for their future choices. They need to explore the world independently to build confidence. Friends also helps them cope with stress and boost self-confidence. When young people use their time with peers who have similar interests they tend to feel more comfortable and better understood. Consequently entertainment activities are more beneficial for their personal development than simply staying at home. In conclusion, I do not agree that teenagers should use most of their time to family. Family relationships are crucial but teenagers also need the freedom to explore their interests and engage in social activities that support their personal growth.

Band 7 Model Answer

It is widely believed that young people should spend most of their free time at home with their families rather than taking part in leisure activities. However, I completely disagree with this view because young people need opportunities to build independence, confidence, and communication skills.

One major reason why this belief is unconvincing is that spending more time at home does not necessarily strengthen family relationships. Most teenagers already see their parents every day and talk to them about school or personal issues. Forcing them to stay at home can make them feel controlled and limit their sense of independence. Young people often have different interests from their parents, so spending too much time together may feel repetitive and uninteresting. As a result, extended family time does not always lead to meaningful connections.

Another reason why I disagree is that leisure activities outside the home provide essential opportunities for personal growth. Participating in clubs, volunteering, or spending time with peers helps young people develop teamwork, leadership, and communication skills. Exploring new environments also allows them to discover talents that may influence their future goals. In addition, friends play an important role in helping teenagers cope with stress and build self-confidence. When they spend time with peers who share the same interests, they feel more supported and understood. Therefore, entertainment and social activities are often more beneficial for their development than staying at home.

In conclusion, I do not agree that teenagers should spend most of their free time with their families. Although family relationships are important, young people need freedom to explore their interests and engage in social activities that support their personal growth.

🌸 Agree or Disagree Essay Maqsudjon01/12/2025, 10:43:44
Rubric: Many believe that young people should spend more of their free time with their family instead of other entertainment. Do you agree or Disagree? Essay: It is commonly believed that young people should devote the majority of their leisure time to interacting with their family members rather than engaging in entertainment activities. I entairley disagree, mainly because entertainment offers signaficant social development benifits, and the generation gap often makes family interaction less enjoyable or productive for children. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that young people already spend a large part of their day with family members. As a result, asking them to spend even more time at home may become boring and repetitive. For example, family conversations often focus on daily routines, school, or parents’ problems, which may not be interesting for children. This assumption is flawed because teenagers usually prefer hobbies or activities that match their interests, not only family talks. Therefore, I refute the idea that they should devote more free time to their family. Another factor that challenges this claim is that entertainment and social activities help children develop communication and teamwork skills. When they join clubs - sport teams, or recreational groups - they meet new people and learn how to cooperate with others. This clearly demonstrates that social experience outside home is necessary for building confidence and independence. Moreover, taking part in entertainment events allows youngsters to relax after school, which improves their mental wellbeing. For this reason, I strongly believe that limiting entertainment time is not beneficial. In conclusion, I cannot agree with the idea that young people should spend most of their free time with their families. To sum up, entertainment and social environments play a key role in children’s development, so they should be encouraged to explore new hobbies and friendships instead of staying only with family.

Band 7.5 Model Answer

It is commonly believed that young people should devote most of their leisure time to interacting with their families rather than engaging in entertainment activities. I entirely disagree with this view, mainly because entertainment provides important social development benefits, and the generation gap often makes family interaction less enjoyable or meaningful for children.

One major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that young people already spend a considerable part of their day with their families. Asking them to spend even more time at home can easily become repetitive and uninteresting. Family conversations often focus on daily routines, school matters, or parents’ concerns, which may not appeal to teenagers. They generally prefer hobbies and activities that match their personal interests rather than constant family discussions. Therefore, I disagree with the idea that young people should devote the majority of their free time to family interactions.

Another strong argument supporting this view is that entertainment and social activities help children develop essential communication and teamwork skills. When they join clubs, sports teams, or recreational groups, they meet new people and learn how to cooperate with others. Such experiences outside the home are necessary for building confidence and independence. Moreover, engaging in enjoyable activities allows young people to relax after school, which supports their mental well-being. For these reasons, limiting their entertainment time would be counterproductive.

In conclusion, I do not agree that young people should spend most of their free time with their families. Entertainment and social environments play a key role in children’s personal development, and they should be encouraged to explore new hobbies and friendships rather than remain solely within the family circle.

Band 7 Model Answer

Nowadays, it is commonly believed that young people should spend more of their free time with their families rather than on entertainment. I completely agree with this view, mainly because family time plays a crucial role in emotional development, building essential skills, and supporting long-term well-being.

One key reason why this view is valid is that family interactions help strengthen young people’s social values. Spending time with parents and siblings teaches communication skills, empathy, and responsibility, which are difficult to develop through entertainment alone. For example, sharing meals or having meaningful conversations creates an atmosphere of trust and emotional security, especially during challenging periods in adolescence. These experiences not only improve family relationships but also reduce behavioural problems.

Another strong argument supporting this idea is that family time shapes young people’s personality and future decision-making. Parents often provide guidance based on real-life experience, helping their children make wiser choices about education, relationships, and career paths. Although entertainment activities are enjoyable, they cannot replace the wisdom and support that families offer. Furthermore, in today’s digital age, where many young people are distracted by screens, prioritising family time can help maintain balance and protect emotional health.

In conclusion, although entertainment has its benefits, I am convinced that spending more leisure time with family is far more meaningful. Family gatherings provide life guidance, emotional support, and essential social skills — advantages that entertainment alone cannot offer.

🌸 Unknown01/12/2025, 07:00:36
*Many believe that young people should spend more of their free time with their family instead of other entertainment. Do you agree or disagree?*

Nowadays it is becoming popular among teenagers spend less time in family circle. This essay argues that this view is misguided because although family time is important, young people also need personal space to develop socially and emotionally.

A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that while spending time with relatives can be valuable, teenagers also need opportunities to explore the world beyond their home environment. Activities such as sports, hobbies, or spending time with friends allow them to build independence, improve teamwork, and develop communication skills that they cannot gain at home.

Another factor that challenges this claim is that modern entertainment such as playing games, attending clubs, or participating in online communities can help young people explore their interests and creativity. For instance, joining a music group or playing competitive sports not only provides enjoyment but also teaches discipline and helps them relieve stress after school. Moreover, constantly being required to prioritise family time may create unnecessary pressure, especially if the young person feels their need for autonomy is being overlooked. Healthy development includes learning to balance responsibilities and personal freedom.

Inconclusion, although family time is valuable, I believe that young people should not be expected to spend the majority of their free time at home. Allowing them to participate in various entertainment activities outside the family contributes to their social skills, emotional health, and overall personal growth, while also helping them gain broader experiences that shape a more confident and independent personality.

⭐ FINAL BAND: 6.0–6.5

You are very close to Band 7 — you only need cleaner grammar and more natural vocabulary.

Band 7 Model Answer

Nowadays, many teenagers spend less time with their families, and this trend is becoming increasingly common. This essay argues that although family time is important, young people also need personal space to develop socially and emotionally.

One major reason why the belief that teenagers should spend most of their free time with their families is unconvincing is that young people need opportunities to explore the world beyond their home environment. Activities such as sports, hobbies, or meeting friends allow them to build independence, improve teamwork, and strengthen communication skills that cannot always be developed at home. These experiences play a vital role in helping teenagers understand themselves and interact with others more effectively.

Another factor that challenges this claim is that modern forms of entertainment, such as gaming, joining clubs, or participating in online communities, allow young people to explore their interests and creativity. For example, joining a music group or playing competitive sports not only provides enjoyment but also teaches discipline and helps them relieve stress after school. In contrast, constantly being required to prioritise family time may create unnecessary pressure, particularly if teenagers feel that their need for autonomy is not respected. A healthy upbringing requires a balance between responsibility and personal freedom.

In conclusion, although spending time with family is valuable, young people should not be expected to spend most of their free time at home. Allowing them to take part in a variety of recreational activities contributes to their social development, emotional well-being, and overall personal growth, while giving them broader experiences that help shape a confident and independent personality.

Some people hold the view that children should spend most of their leisure time with their family members rather than engaging in recreational activities. This essay argues that this view is misguided because the benefits of leisure-time activities are more significant. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that leisure time activities play a crucial role in childrens physical and mental development. When children engage in activities such as sports, art, or outdoor play, they not only improve their physical health but also enxance essential skills like creativity, teamwork, and problem-solving. These opportunities are often unavailable at home, where children may spend their time passively. Moreover, particepating in recreational activities allows children to socialise with their peers, which helps them build confidence and develop stronger communication skills. Therefore, limiting their free time solely to family interactions may hinder their overall growth. Another factor challenges this claim is that recreational activities can make children happier and more relaxed. When children play games, draw, or join clubs, they can enjoy themselves and forget about stress from school. This helps them feel better and also improves their health a little. If children spend all their free time only with family, they may feel bored or tired of the same routine. Therefore, having time for different activities is important to make children’s life more interesting and enjoyable. In conclusion, while spending time with family is important, children should also have time for recreational activities. These activities help them develop important skills, become more confident, and enjoy their free time.

Band 7 Model Answer

Some people believe that children should spend most of their free time with their families rather than engaging in recreational activities. However, this essay argues that this view is misguided because leisure-time activities play a more important role in children’s development.

One major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that leisure-time activities are essential for children’s physical and mental growth. When children participate in sports, art, or outdoor play, they not only strengthen their physical health but also develop key skills such as creativity, teamwork, and problem-solving. These opportunities are often limited at home, where children may spend their time passively. Furthermore, taking part in recreational activities allows children to socialise with peers, which helps them build confidence and improve communication skills. Therefore, restricting their free time to family interactions alone may hinder their overall development.

Another factor that challenges this claim is that recreational activities contribute to children’s emotional well-being. Activities such as drawing, playing games, or joining hobby clubs allow children to relax and release stress from school. This supports their mental health and helps them maintain a positive mood. In contrast, spending all their free time with family may become repetitive and less stimulating. As a result, having access to a variety of enjoyable activities is important for making children’s daily life more balanced and fulfilling.

In conclusion, although family time is valuable, children should also have the freedom to engage in recreational activities. Such experiences help them develop essential skills, build confidence, and maintain emotional well-being.

🌸 AKRAMJONOV MUSLIMBEK agree disagree30/11/2025, 08:28:55

Rubric:

Many believe that young people should spend more of their free time with their family instead of other entertainment.

Do you agree or disagree?

Essay:

It is commonly believed that young people ought to allocate most of their spare time to their family rather than to entertainment activities. This essay argues that this view is misguided because advantages of entertainment are more significant.


A major reason why this argument unconvincing

is that family reunions are boring and repetetive for many youngsters, because they already spend much time at home talking or interacting with family members. Additional communication through family gatherings might be uninteresting for children, as topics are repetitive, such as authorities or world issues which are interesting only for adults. This burden creates pressure and restricts personal growth. Therefore, I disagree the view that family reunions are significant and teenagers should devote their leisure time.

Another factor that challenges this

claim is that entertainment can be educational. Since, not all entertainment activities are useless, as many of them improve vital skills, such as logical thinking. A compelling example of this is strategy-based video games, such as Clash of Clans or Dota 2 which encourage players to analyze complex situations and make decisions under pressure or playing a musical instrument, which improves creativity by encouraging them to learn different sounds and create own musical patterns. These activities let them wind down. Thus, entertainment is beneficial for developing necessary skills.


To sum up, I completely disagree with the view that young people should devote most of their free time interacting with family due to differences in interests and vital skills that can be learned by engaging in entertainment if used in moderation.

Band 7 Model Answer

Many people believe that young individuals should spend most of their free time with their families rather than engaging in entertainment activities. However, I disagree with this view because entertainment provides important benefits that cannot be replaced by family interaction alone.

One major reason why this opinion is unconvincing is that family gatherings can sometimes be repetitive and uninteresting for teenagers. Young people already spend a considerable amount of time at home talking to their parents, so additional family meetings may feel unnecessary. Moreover, the topics discussed during these gatherings—such as politics or world events—are often more appealing to adults than to teenagers. As a result, young people may feel bored or pressured, which can limit their personal growth. Therefore, I do not believe that they should devote most of their free time to family interactions.

Another factor that challenges this view is that many forms of entertainment are educational and help develop important skills. Not all entertainment activities are meaningless; for example, strategy-based video games such as Clash of Clans and Dota 2 encourage players to analyse complex situations and make decisions under pressure. Similarly, learning a musical instrument enhances creativity and discipline, as teenagers must practise regularly and create their own musical patterns. These activities also help them relax after academic stress. Thus, entertainment can play a key role in developing useful cognitive and creative skills.

In conclusion, I completely disagree with the idea that young people should spend the majority of their free time with their families. Differences in interests and the valuable skills gained through entertainment show that young people benefit greatly from engaging in enjoyable activities, as long as these are used in moderation.

I completely disagree with the claim that social media creates a sence of social disconnection because of things that it offers. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that social media strengthens relationships by allowing people to communicate across long distances. Many platforms now give opportunity to not only chat via internet but also helps make video calls. Individuals such as people with physical or mental impairments who could not attend school or university, study online from home which makes life even more easier for them. It is not only useful for people with disabilities but additionally for students who study abroad. For instance learners often experience homesickness in foreign country so internet platforms give opportunity to contact with relatives and place video calls which can replace a real life conversations. Another factor that challenges this claim is that online platforms help users find supportive communities and collaborate with others who share similar interests. Online platforms allow people to join groups based on shared interest, educational goals or personal challenges. For example students who prepare for exams or which share same hobbies could exchange ideas and provide advice for each others which is not easy even offline. In addition social media not only connects people but creates meaningful collaborations, challenging the idea which could lead to isolation. In conclusion, I entirely disagree with the view that social networking sites lead people to become less social,its opportunities to most of the people especially for disables. For these reasons, I strongly believe that it has more advantages.

Band 7 Model Answer

It is commonly believed that internet-based communication platforms reduce face-to-face communication and weaken social relationships. However, I completely disagree with this view, as social media offers many opportunities to maintain connections and build supportive communities.

One major reason why social media does not isolate people is that it strengthens relationships across long distances. Modern platforms not only allow users to chat online but also provide high-quality video calls. This is especially beneficial for individuals with physical or mental disabilities who may find it difficult to attend school or university in person; online communication enables them to study from home and stay socially involved. It is also extremely helpful for students who study abroad. Many of them experience homesickness, and social media allows them to talk to relatives and video-call their families, helping them stay emotionally connected even while living in another country.

Another reason why social media supports social connection is that it helps people find communities and collaborate with others who share similar interests. Online groups allow users to join discussions based on hobbies, educational goals, or personal experiences. For example, students preparing for exams or people with the same hobbies can exchange ideas, give feedback, and support one another. These interactions are often more convenient than offline communication and help users feel included. In this way, social media promotes meaningful collaboration and challenges the belief that it leads to isolation.

In conclusion, I strongly disagree with the view that social networking sites make people less social. These platforms help individuals maintain relationships, support people with disabilities, and create new opportunities to connect. Therefore, social media has far more advantages than disadvantages.

Some individuals are of the opinion that social media platforms make people more isolated nowadays, while others believe that these apps actually help us become more connected. Some people think that spending time online only reduce real communication, whereas others claim that social networks improve relationships and make life easier. This essay will look into both views and explains why I believe that social media makes many people more isolated than before. On the one hand, there is a belief that social media connect people from different parts of the world. Many users argue that they can talk to friends and family far away without any problem, so social networks are very useful. It is undeniable that the most significant benefit of social media is that communication become faster and more comfortable. However some people says that online platforms help shy people to communicate better, but this argument is not always true. On the other hand, I would argue that social media actually bring more harm to real life communication. Advocates of this view argue that people spend too much time scrolling posts instead of meeting friends face to face. For example, many teenagers feel lonely and depressed even if they have hundreds of followers, which shows that online connection is not real. While social media can be entertaining sometimes, I am convinced that these platforms create distance between people in long-term. Those who support this idea believe that too much online time worsen family relationships and cause mental problems. In conclusion, although both sides have strong argumments, I would like to say that social media platforms make people more isolated than connected. Summing up, there is no doubt that social networks have some benefits, but depending on them completly would be a serious mistake for many individuals.

Rubric: social media platforms make people more isolated rather than more connected. Do you agree or disagree? However, I completely disagree with the claim that this makes individuals to feel isolated,as i believe they offere meaningful opportunities for communication. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that social media does not isolate people is that it helps maintain close relationships despite physical distance. Platforms such as messaging apps like Instagram or telegram allows individuals to communicate instantly with friends and family, even if they live in different countries. Consequently, these digital interactions help reduce loneliness instead of increasing it.
