Band 7 Version (Improved)

Many people argue that social media platforms cause users to feel separated rather than united. However, I completely disagree with the claim that they make individuals feel isolated, as I believe they offer meaningful opportunities for communication.

A major reason why this opinion is unconvincing is that social media helps people maintain close relationships despite physical distance. Platforms such as Instagram or Telegram allow individuals to communicate instantly with friends and family, even if they live in different countries. By sharing everyday experiences, providing emotional support, and celebrating important events online, users often feel more connected rather than distant from their loved ones.

Another factor that challenges this claim is that social media provides opportunities to form new connections and join supportive communities. People can engage in online groups that match their interests, such as art, fitness or education, and interact with others who share similar passions. This is especially beneficial for individuals who struggle with face-to-face communication, as online environments create a comfortable space for self-expression. Consequently, these digital interactions help reduce loneliness instead of increasing it.

In conclusion, although some argue that social media leads to isolation, I firmly believe that it is more beneficial. These platforms strengthen existing relationships and create new social opportunities, helping people feel more connected and supported in their daily lives.

In recent years, social media has become one of the most influential forms of communication. While these platforms were originally designed to connect individuals across the globe, it is often argued that they actually make people more isolated. I completely agree with the view that despite offering opportunities for communication, social media often contributes to greater loneliness and weaker real-life relationships. One key reason why this view is valid is that, social media creates an illusion of connection. People may have hundreds of online friends or followers, yet these relationships are often not strong. Instead of talking face-to-face, individuals exchange short messages, likes, or emojis, which cannot replace real interaction. As a result, some users spend less time with family or friends in person, which eventually leads to emotional distance and isolation. Moreover, extensive use of social networks can negatively affect mental health. People often compare one’s life with their online can cause feelings of anxiety or depression. When people feel discouraged, they tend to avoid real-life social activities, further deepening their sense of loneliness. As a result, social media can push individuals away from native human contact. Another strong argument supporting this idea is that, when online interaction replaces real-world socialising, people may gradually lose communication skills and become more dependent on virtual contact. Afterwords, this dependence can make individuals feel more isolated, even while they constantly connected. In conclusion, I agree with the view that it often makes people more isolated. Its tendency to create shallow relationships and reduce real-life communication have strong negative effect on young generation. To avoid isolation, social media should be used in moderation and just being an additional part of offline interactions.

Band 7 Model Answer

In recent years, social media has become one of the most influential forms of communication. While these platforms were originally designed to connect individuals across the globe, many argue that they actually make people more isolated. I completely agree with this view, as social media often contributes to loneliness and weaker real-life relationships.

One key reason why social media can increase isolation is that it creates an illusion of connection. People may have hundreds of online friends or followers, yet these relationships are often superficial. Instead of engaging in face-to-face conversations, users exchange short messages, likes, or emojis, which cannot replace real interaction. As a result, individuals may spend less time with family and friends in person, leading to emotional distance and loneliness. In addition, the excessive use of social networks can negatively affect mental health. People frequently compare their lives with the idealised posts of others, which can lead to feelings of anxiety or low self-esteem. When this happens, they may avoid real-life social activities, further strengthening the cycle of isolation.

Another strong argument supporting this view is that replacing real-world interaction with online communication can weaken essential social skills. If people rely too heavily on virtual contact, they may struggle to express themselves confidently in person or maintain meaningful conversations. Over time, this dependence can make individuals feel more isolated, even if they appear active online.

In conclusion, I believe that social media often makes people more isolated. Its tendency to create shallow relationships and reduce face-to-face communication has negative effects, especially on young people. To avoid these outcomes, social media should be used in moderation and treated as a support to real-life interactions rather than a replacement for them.

VK, for example, is well-known not only like a platform for having talk with acquaintances but also prone to entail particular feed for world news. Teacher Feedback
📌 1. Task Response — Band 6

Strengths
• You gave a clear opinion (you disagree).
• You provided two main reasons.
• You attempted to give examples.

Weaknesses
• Your arguments are not fully developed.
• Some examples are incorrect or irrelevant:
• Pinterest is NOT used “to stay in touch with friends” – incorrect example.
• The introduction contains unclear meaning (“advantages ways”, “whenever users are”).
• The conclusion is very long and grammatically incorrect.

So although you answered the question, the quality of ideas is inconsistent → Band 6.

⸻

📌 2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 5

Strengths
• Paragraphs are used.
• You attempt topic sentences.

Problems
• Many sentences are unclear or illogical.
• Linking words are misused.
• Ideas inside paragraphs are not organised logically.
• The text is difficult to follow due to grammar errors.

Examples of unclear / incoherent sentences:
• “created convenient method to make users together for enhancing existing relationships”
• “Pinterest, which is designed for amateurs who aid to stay in touch…”
• “a hundred of billions individuals claim, that majority platforms promote…”

The examiner will struggle to understand several parts → Band 5.

⸻

📌 3. Lexical Resource — Band 5

This is the main problem.

There are many incorrect or unnatural collocations, incorrect word choices, and spelling mistakes:

❌ Wrong or unnatural vocabulary
• “advantages ways” → advantageous ways
• “establishing new relationships” → okay, but repetitive
• “global occasion” → global events
• “convenient method to make users together” → incorrect
• “amateurs who aid to stay in touch” → incorrect
• “expand subscriber’s network” → unnatural
• “political, social, economical situation” → economic
• “remain connection with world” → remain connected to the world
• “prone to entail” → completely incorrect
• “segregation” → too strong and wrong meaning in this context
• “favorable for users in point of creating” → unnatural

This level of inaccuracy keeps LR at Band 5.

⸻

📌 4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5

There are many frequent grammar errors, including:

❌ Articles
• “individual’s sociability” → individuals’ sociability
• “a clear example such as Pinterest” → wrong structure
• “a hundred of billions individuals” → incorrect

❌ Subject–verb agreement
• “media platforms can also have vital effects” → okay
• but many others are incorrect.

❌ Sentence structure errors
• Run-on sentences
• Fragments
• Missing commas
• Incorrect relative clause use

❌ Preposition mistakes
• “sharing photos, emotions and life updates, alternatively” → wrongly used
• “connect with others peoples” → other people

There are errors in nearly every sentence → Band 5.

⸻

⭐ FINAL BAND: 5.0 – 5.5

Band 7 Model Essay

Nowadays, many people believe that social networking sites have a strong influence on people’s social behaviour. I completely disagree with the view that these platforms isolate individuals. In my opinion, social media helps people maintain relationships, create new connections, and stay informed about important events around the world.

One major reason why social media does not cause isolation is that it allows people to stay in touch even when they are far apart. In the past, long-distance friendships were difficult to maintain, but today applications such as Telegram and Instagram make communication quick, simple, and affordable. For instance, students who study abroad can easily talk to their families every day through video calls and messages. This constant interaction strengthens bonds and reduces feelings of loneliness.

Furthermore, social media encourages the formation of new friendships and communities. People with similar interests can join online groups where they share ideas and learn from one another. For example, someone who is interested in start-ups can join entrepreneurship forums, receive feedback on their projects, and connect with people who share the same goals. As a result, users feel more included and supported, rather than isolated.

In addition, social networking sites play an important role in increasing people’s awareness of global events. Most platforms provide updates on political, social, economic, and cultural issues from around the world. This continuous flow of information helps users stay informed and participate in discussions that unite people around shared concerns. For example, platforms like VK and Instagram offer news feeds that allow individuals to follow international events in real time.

In conclusion, although some people believe that social media separates individuals, I strongly disagree. These platforms help people maintain strong relationships, make new connections, and stay informed about global developments. Therefore, social media brings people closer rather than isolating them.

Rubric: Social media platforms make people more isolated rather than more connected. Do you agree or disagree? Essay: Social media has become one of the main tools for communication. Some people hold the view that these platforms make individuals more isolated, but i strongly oppose the view. In my view, social media helps people stay connected, build communities, and even improve cooperation for study or work. A major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that social media allows people maintain relationship even when they are physically far apart. In the past, long distance friendships were difficult to keep, but now applications - Telegram and Instagram - make communication quick and free. A clear counterexample can be seen in between students which are studying abroad, they can easily talk to their family every day through video calls.This constant interaction prevents loneliness and strengthens borders between people. Furthermore, social media encourages the formation of new friendships. People with similar throughts and interests can join online groups and communities. Someone, for instance, who loves doing start ups can join start ups group, learn from others, share their works, and take a feedbacks. As a result, they feel more included rather than isolated. In addition, social media can be used as a collobrative tool for education and work. Students often create group chat to discuss homework, share ideas, and help each other. During the Covid-19 pandemic, many schools continued teaching through online platforms, which shows that social media can actually bring people closer while studying together. In conclusion, i cannot agree with the idea that social media reduces real communication, i think it does the opposite. It helps individuals maintain relationships, make new friendships, and colloborate more effectively. Therefore i strongly believe that social media connects us more than it isolates us.

• Run-on sentences

⭐ OVERALL: 6.0–6.5

BAND 7 essay:

Social media has become one of the main tools for communication. Some people believe that these platforms make individuals more isolated, but I strongly disagree. In my view, social media helps people stay connected, build communities, and collaborate more effectively for study or work.

One major reason why this argument is unconvincing is that social networking platforms allow people to maintain relationships even when they are physically far apart. In the past, long-distance friendships were difficult to keep, but applications such as Telegram and Instagram make communication quick and free. A clear example can be seen among students studying abroad who can easily talk to their families every day through video calls. This constant interaction prevents loneliness and strengthens bonds between people.

Furthermore, social media encourages the formation of new friendships. People with similar interests can join online groups and communities. For instance, someone who enjoys working on start-ups can join entrepreneurship groups, learn from others, share their projects, and receive feedback. As a result, they feel more included rather than isolated.

In addition, social media can be used as a collaborative tool for education and work. Students often create group chats to discuss homework, share ideas, and support one another. During the Covid-19 pandemic, many schools continued teaching through online platforms, which shows that social media can help people stay connected while studying together.

In conclusion, I do not agree with the idea that social media reduces real communication. Instead, it helps individuals maintain relationships, form new friendships, and collaborate more effectively. Therefore, I strongly believe that social media connects us far more than it isolates us.

🌸 Do you agree or disagree? AKRAMJONOV MUSLIMBEK28/11/2025, 08:36:25
Rubrik: Social media platforms make people more isolated rather than more connected. Do you agree or disagree? Essay:
There is a growing belief that social media causes isolation instead of connection with people. I completely agree with the view that social media has a negative impact. One key reason why this view is valid is that social networking platforms may cause addiction. These platforms, such as Instagram or TikTok, are attractive enough to keep users engaged for a long time, since they are designed for this with endless scroll of short videos. While watching these short videos they change mood very quickly. Therefore, after some time of watching, it might be boring for them to do usual activities, as brain gets used to fast changes. Although these social networks are good source of information, many use it not in moderation, so this leads to information overload. As a result, young people will be less sensitive. Another strong argument supporting this idea is that excessive use of social media reduces face-to-face communication between individuals, as it causes less personal interaction. This clearly demonstrates that people might have fewer friends and they may feel loneliness. In addition, this may lead to watch more and more videos, since they do not have any friends to hang out with. A compelling example of this is when family reunites, many will be glued to phone screen while waiting for food. Therefore, these reunions might be reduced, since they will not be as interesting as before.
To sum up, I firmly support the view that social networking platforms are affecting society more negatively than positively due to addiction and less communication.

• Arguments jump from one idea to another without strong logical links.
• Some claims need clearer development.
• Conclusion is very short and does not summarise the main points.

Still a Band 6 because you answered the question fully, but not deeply or precisely enough.

• Clear paragraphing
• Logical order of ideas
• Each paragraph has a main point

❌ Weaknesses

• Repeated structures in paragraphs.
• Some sentences feel disconnected.
• “Therefore” and “as a result” used even when logic is not strong.

Good structure, but internal cohesion needs improvement.

📌 3. Lexical Resource – Band 5.5

• “endless scroll of short videos” → endless scrolling of short videos
• “usual activities” → vague
• “brain gets used to fast changes” → simplistic
• “less sensitive” → unclear meaning
• “causes less personal interaction” → awkward phrasing
• “they may feel loneliness” → feel lonely
• “phone screen” → their phone screens
• “reunions might be reduced” → unnatural
• “affecting society more negatively than positively” → acceptable but weak

❌ Repetition:
• “short videos” (too many times)
• “people”, “social media”, “these platforms”

You need more precise and natural collocations.

⸻

📌 4. Grammar Range & Accuracy – Band 5.5

❌ Frequent errors:
• Missing articles: “These platforms are attractive enough” → okay, but later many are missing
• Incorrect sentence structure
• “While watching these short videos they change mood very quickly” (who is “they”?)
• Wrong cause–effect grammar:
• “this may lead to watch more videos” → lead them to watch
• Inconsistent singular/plural:
• “phone screen”
• Word forms:
• “good source” not “good source of information but many use it not in moderation” → needs restructuring

You produce some good sentences, but too many inaccuracies remain for Band 7.

⸻

⭐ Overall: 5.5–6.0

You have Band 7 ideas but Band 6 accuracy.

Band 7 Model

There is a growing belief that social media isolates people rather than connecting them. I completely agree with this view because social networking platforms often create addiction and reduce meaningful face-to-face communication.

One key reason why social media can lead to isolation is that these platforms are intentionally designed to keep users online for long periods. Applications like Instagram and TikTok use endless scrolling and short, fast-paced videos that constantly stimulate the brain. As a result, many users find it difficult to enjoy slower, everyday activities because they become used to quick entertainment. Although social media can be a useful source of information, people often consume it without moderation, which leads to information overload and emotional fatigue.

Another strong argument is that excessive use of social media reduces personal interaction. Many individuals spend so much time online that they have fewer opportunities to build real friendships in their daily lives. For instance, during family gatherings, it is common to see people focusing on their phones instead of talking to one another. Over time, this weakens relationships and makes social events less meaningful, causing people to feel more isolated. In addition, online communication usually involves short messages or emojis, which cannot replace genuine conversations.

In conclusion, I firmly believe that social media has a negative influence on society because it encourages addictive behaviour and reduces real-life communication.

Many people prefer to chat online, watch short videos, or scroll through posts instead of meeting their friends or family in person. Because of this, they miss real convercations where they can clearly express their feelings and understand others. For example, many teenagers sit together in the same room, but each of them is busy with their phones, so they do not actually talk to each other. Over time, this reduces their ability to build strong reletionships and makes them feel more lonely. 📌 1. Task Response — Band 6

• The conclusion is too short and doesn’t summarise properly.
• Some sentences are unclear or incomplete.

➡ You answered the task, but not deeply or precisely enough for Band 7.


📌 2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 6

Strengths:
• Clear paragraph structure.
• Logical flow of ideas.

Problems:
• Some sentences are not linked smoothly.
• Repetition of “one reason… another reason…” structure (mechanical).
• Weak linking in some places due to grammar errors.

➡ Good structure but needs more natural cohesion.


📌 3. Lexical Resource — Band 5.5

This is your biggest weakness.

Many spelling mistakes:
• convercations → conversations
• reletionships → relationships
• interiaction → interaction
• folowers → followers

Many unnatural or incorrect collocations:
• “real-life interiaction”
• “many friendships becoming more fake”
• “not strong friendships”
• “less communicating”
• “spend less time communicating in real life” (okay, but repeated too much)

Informal / spoken language:
• “not good enough”
• “deeply talk”
• “fake friendships”
• “busy with their phones”

➡ You need more precise academic vocabulary.


📌 4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5

You make very frequent grammar errors, especially with:

❌ Articles
• “Its commonly believed” → It is commonly believed
• “this make them feel” → this makes them feel

❌ Subject–verb agreement
• “friendships becoming” → friendships are becoming
• “this make them feel” → makes

❌ Sentence fragments / missing punctuation
• “Therefore, social media seems to connect people, it often reduces real-life interiaction” → comma splice

❌ Incorrect sentence structure
• “Therefore, social media seems to connect people, it often reduces…” → should be Although social media seems to connect people, it often reduces…

➡ Grammar errors appear in almost every sentence → Band 5.


⭐ OVERALL: 5.5 – 6.0

Your ideas are good, but your accuracy pulls you down.
The good news: your structure and content are already Band 7-level.

Band 7 Model Essay

It is commonly believed that social media makes people more isolated rather than more connected. I completely agree with this view because online communication often replaces real-life interaction and many relationships formed on social media are weak or superficial.

One key reason why social media can increase isolation is that it reduces the amount of time people spend communicating face-to-face. Many individuals prefer to chat online, watch videos or scroll through posts instead of meeting friends or family in person. As a result, they miss real conversations where they can clearly express their feelings and understand others. For instance, it is common to see teenagers sitting in the same room but focusing only on their phones instead of talking to each other. Over time, this weakens their ability to build strong relationships and leads to greater feelings of loneliness.

Another strong argument is that many friendships on social media are not very meaningful. People usually share only the best parts of their lives online, which creates unrealistic expectations and makes others feel dissatisfied with their own situation. In addition, online communication often relies on short messages or emojis, so people rarely discuss serious topics or offer emotional support. For example, someone may have hundreds of followers but still lack a close friend to talk to when they face a problem. Consequently, these shallow online connections can make people feel even more isolated.

In conclusion, social media often increases isolation because it reduces real-life interactions and encourages weak, surface-level relationships. For these reasons, I believe that social media can make people feel more lonely rather than truly connected.

🌸 Sevinch Turopova27/11/2025, 02:08:31
Rubric: some students take a year off between school and university,to work or to trevel. Do the advantages of this outweigh the disadvantages? Over the last few years a gap year is taken between schools and higher education among some students, with purpose of working or traveling. While i accept that this changes can have certain benefits for students. I believe that it is likely to have a harmful impact on their education. On one hand, there is some positive aspects for taking a year off. A distinct advantage is that students can unwind after a years of education and it helps them to reduce stress levels. Moreover they can develop independence and improve their financial situation.For instance working during the gap year lets students save money for their tuition fees or living expenses and it makes students more responsible and confident. On the other hand, I believe this development has far more drawbacks. Major disadvantage is that it has a risk of immotivation and losing interest of studying at university. Sinse a long break might make it harder to return to studying or students might decide not to go to university at all. Another point to consider is the finances and falling behind their peers. Travelling can be expensive and and may lead to dabt. While their peers can start their education earlier and graduate earlier, students who chose a gap year may not pass the exams and the pressure of their debt and regration will couse depression or even suicide. To sum up, while a year off can be good due to mental rest and developing independence, ut can also damage their mental health and reduce motivation

THE WINNER TAKES IT ALL
🌸 Do the ADV outweigh DISADV? Maqsudjon26/11/2025, 11:13:20
Rubrik: Some students take a year off betwen school and university, to work or to travel. Do the advantages outweigh to disadvantages? Essay: Many students choose to take gap year between finishing school and starting university to work employment and travelling purposes. In my opinion gap year could more benifitional than drowbacks. One the one hand, taking a year of can lead to several disadvantages. One major problem is that students who start working and earn a regular income may become less motivated to continue their education, financial independence may seem more appealing than academic pressure. Moreover, if the gap year poorly planned, valuable time could be wasted, and instead of gaining new skills, teenagers might spend their days on unproductive activities such as relaxation or intertainment. This situation is often viewed negativly by parents and educators because it offers little long term benefit.Additionally, returning to academic life after a gap year can feel burden, as study habits may have weakened, and students may struggle to adabt to structured learning environment again. One the other hand, a gap year also offers significant advantages. Firstly, it can provide young people with real world experience that cannot always be gained in the classroom.Working part time or volunteering may help students learn responsibility, manage money, and developing communication skills. Secondly, travelling during the gap year can expand students horizons. They might be explore new countries, learn foreign languages, experience different cultures. As a result, they often become more independent and mature, returning to university with clearer goals and stronger motivation. In many cases, this break from formal study allows them to enter higher education with confidence and fresh mindset. In conclusion, while a gap year carries risk such as loss of motivation and wasted time, it can also serve as a valuable period for personal growth and development. While proper planning, a gap year may become a productive experience that prepares students for universtiy life both emotionally and intelectually.

Nowadays it is increasingly common for people to study higher education overseas. While the proposal has some potential drawbacks, I believe the benefits carry more weight overall.


On the one hand studying overseas can be pricy. Tuition fees, accommodation and travel costs are higher than in a student’s home country which can be financial pressure students and their family. Students may also struggle with homesickness and culture shock. They may feel lonely and cannot adapt to a new country, especially the culture and lifestyle. Difference language can be challanging for everyday communication. Some students may also find difficult to make friends at first everything can be unfamiliar. But some  students are supports by university services to help them adapt. These support systems can play a important role to helping students. 


On the other hand, the benefits of studying overseas are significant. They often provide better opportunities, advanced facilities and courses that not avalible in the home country. This advantages can better enhance a student’s knowledge and skills. Students can also build connections with people from around the world, which can benefit their future careers. the experience can help them improve their language skills and broaden their worldview. Living abroad also encourage independence and confidence. Students learn to solve problems on their own in a new environment. 


To sum up, despite some negative aspects, I believe that the benefits of studying overseas are more significant for the reasons aforementioned. It can be valuable in a student’s personal and professional development

🌸 Farrukh31/12/1969, 16:00:00
Some individuals are of the opinion that global warming is the most pressing environmental problem nowadays, while others believe that deforestation has a more devastating impact on our world. Some people think that rising temperatures is the biggest danger for humans, whereas others claim that cutting down forests effect the planet much more serious.

On the one hand, there is a belief that global warming bring more harm than any other problem. Many scientists argue that increasing greenhouse gases cause melting ice caps, rising sea levels and extreme weathers, so this issue must be taken very serious. It is undeniable that the most significant result of global warming is that millions of people may lose their homes in the future. However some people says that global warming is too slow process to worry now, but this argument is sometimes not true.

On the other hand, I would argue that deforestation also has huge negative impacts which cannot be ignored. Advocates of this view argue that cutting forests destroys biodiversity, animals lose their habitats and the air become more polluted. For example, without trees, the Earth cannot produce enough oxygen very quick. While some countries try to plant new trees, I am convinced that these efforts are not enough for long-term protecting the nature. Those who support this idea believe that deforestation is the main reason of many environmental problems today.

In conclusion, although both sides have strong argumments, I would like to say that global warming plays a slightly more important role in damaging our planet. Summing up, there is no doubt that deforestation is also dangerous, but ignoring climate change completly would be a huge mistake for humanity.
Word count: 276
Teacher Feedback
1. TASK RESPONSE — Band 6

Strengths
• You discuss both views
• Your opinion is clear
• Examples and explanations are relevant
• The structure of your argument is logical

What lowers the score
• Some ideas are not fully developed
(“global warming is too slow to worry now” → needs deeper explanation)
• The second body paragraph contains inaccurate scientific claims:
• “Earth cannot produce enough oxygen very quick” (not accurate)
• The conclusion repeats the introduction instead of summarising key points
• Some arguments are too general (“huge negative impacts”, “dangerous”)
• You say global warming is “slightly more important”, but your body paragraphs don’t justify “slightly” — this weakens your position

➡ This is a Band 6 level response: fully addresses the task, but lacks precision and depth needed for Band 7.

2. COHERENCE & COHESION — Band 6

Strengths
• Clear paragraph organisation
• Good use of “On the one hand / On the other hand”
• Logical flow of ideas

Weaknesses
• Some linking words are inaccurate:
• “taken very serious”
• “effect the planet much more serious”
• Some sentences do not link smoothly
• A few cohesion errors:
• “However some people says…”
• “Summing up” → too informal for academic writing
• Minor paragraph imbalance (the second paragraph feels shorter and less developed)

➡ Cohesion is good but not fully natural → Band 6.

3. LEXICAL RESOURCE — Band 5.5

You try to use topic vocabulary, but there are many incorrect collocations and inaccurate word choices.

❌ Incorrect / unnatural vocabulary & collocations:
• global warming bring → brings
• very serious → very seriously
• taken very serious → taken very seriously
• cutting down forests effect the planet → affects
• more serious → more seriously
• huge negative impacts → vague
• very quick → quickly
• long-term protecting the nature → long-term protection of nature
• the main reason of many environmental problems → a major cause of many environmental problems

❌ Repetition
• “dangerous”, “negative impact”, “serious”, “harm” — used repeatedly and vaguely

Strengths
• Good topic vocabulary: greenhouse gases, rising sea levels, biodiversity, habitats, climate change
• Good awareness of academic tone

However, Band 7 requires accurate, flexible vocabulary, not just topic words.

➡ Band 5.5 for Lexical Resource.

4. GRAMMAR RANGE & ACCURACY — Band 5

This is the main issue holding your score down.

❌ Frequent errors:
• Subject–verb agreement:
• rising temperatures is → are
• global warming bring → brings
• Articles:
• a important role → an important role
• Verb forms:
• effect → affect
• says → say
• Word forms:
• argumments → arguments
• completly → completely
• Prepositions:
• reason of → reason for
• Sentence structure issues:
• cutting down forests effect the planet much more serious
• air become more polluted

Strengths
• Some correct complex sentences
• You attempt a variety of structures

But Band 7 requires mostly error-free sentences, which is not the case here.

➡ Band 5 for GRA.

OVERALL: 5.5

Rewritten essay for Band 7:

Some individuals believe that global warming is the most urgent environmental problem nowadays, while others argue that deforestation has a more devastating impact on our world. Some people think that rising temperatures pose the greatest danger to humanity, whereas others claim that cutting down forests affects the planet even more seriously.

On the one hand, many people argue that global warming causes more harm than any other environmental issue. Scientists state that increasing greenhouse gas emissions lead to melting ice caps, rising sea levels and more frequent extreme weather events. These consequences show why global warming must be taken very seriously. One of the most significant risks is that millions of people living in coastal regions may lose their homes in the future. Although some people say that global warming is a slow process and not an immediate threat, this view underestimates the long-term effects that are already becoming visible.

On the other hand, some people believe that deforestation has equally serious impacts that should not be ignored. Supporters of this view argue that cutting down forests destroys biodiversity, causes animals to lose their habitats and makes the air more polluted. Trees absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen, so removing large areas of forest reduces the Earth’s ability to regulate the atmosphere. While some countries try to plant new trees, these efforts are often too slow to balance the rapid rate of deforestation. Many people therefore consider deforestation to be a major cause of several environmental problems today.

In conclusion, although deforestation has severe consequences, I believe that global warming is a more pressing issue because of its global scale and long-lasting effects. Ignoring climate change completely would be a serious mistake for humanity, and urgent action is required.
AI Feedback
🌸
🌸 Sevinch Turopova31/12/1969, 16:00:00
Rubric: More and more students are choosing to study at colleges and universities in a foreign country. Do the benefits of studying abroad outweigh the drawbacks? Thanks to the world being more connected, there are now more opportunities for people to go and travel overseas.Institutions in another country are chose to study by an increasing numbers of students. This essay will clarify both sides of the issue and demonstrate my views that the benefits outweigh the drawbacks. On one hand, studying abroad has some downsides.Firstly, finances and language barriers which most students face while they are abroad. Of course, their colleges or universities may offer their oven scholarships and grant options. However, this may not be enough to cover all their needs such as tuitions ( if there are) housing and etc.What about language barriers? Even most advanced speakers can come across with this issue as native speakers may speak in more informal language which wasn't taught to them. Moreover students might experience homesickness and cultural shock. Since everything will be different starting from food and architecture to customs and social norms. If it combines with homesickness students may fall to depression. On the other hand, studying in foreign country offers a huge range of impressive opportunities! One of the great strengths of studying overseas is learning the language and culture of your host country while you are leaving there. They can broaden their perspectives on life and also make new friends, plus they will always have lots of stories to tell to their meets in their hometown. Furthermore they may have better study opportunities overseas than they do at home. Gain more deeply specialised knowledge in their chosen area of study. Besides, if they mention that they have studied abroad it will look very good to potential employers when it comes to applying for occupation. As it shows that they possess valuable workplace qualities such as independence initiative and responsibility which they have developed in foreign country. In conclusion, while studying in foreign country can be bad due to finances and homesickness it can also be more beneficial for their future!

🌸 Glazkov Vladimir31/12/1969, 16:00:00
*While some people consider global warming to be the most pressing environmental problem which we have at the moment, others believe that deforestation has a more devastating impact on our world. Discuss both sided and give your opinion* Some people believe that global warming is the most major ecological dilemma, which we facing right now. However, other claim deforestation has more risk to the Earth. Notwithstanding the arguments between this eco-activists groups, i strongly believe that both problems have an equal impact not only on our lives but also even on our planet. There are some reasons why some people hold the view that global warming is more damaging than cutting down trees. For instance, greenhouse gases generated by unlimited car using or by traditional methods of generating energy based on using fuel oil or coal which emits carbone dioxide into the atmosphere. More than 41 billion tons was emitted into the atmosphere in 2024 and this number is increasing year by year on 0.5% which cause global temperature increase. The second significant factor is overpopulation. Almost 9 billion of population will be reached by 2037, but we already have a problem with food and water sources with our current number of people. We are consuming more and more resources are not renewable and which require lots of energy to produce them. On the other hand, opponents of climate change claim that the deforestations has more impact on our daily lives than global warming. Deforestation also has massive negative effect to our planet such as lack of oxygen. Cutting trees interrupts the process of photosynthesis which helps to accumulate oxygen in our atmosphere and it is a factor in the occurrence of many bronchial diseases. Another type of deforestation is wildfires. A lot of wildfires occurs every year due to people oversight. And it is on of the strongest impacts of deforestation, not only woods disappearance but also emission of carbone dioxide into the atmosphere. At the same time, desertification and global warming are equally influencing us. Logically, inhabiting organisms like a human cannot live without forests, also, they cannot live in extreme temperature of the atmosphere. To conclude, we cannot separate these problems. Both of them have negative impacts on our world.

• You address both views.

Problems

• Some arguments are not logically developed.
• Final paragraph adds a new idea (“cannot live without forests”) instead of concluding.
• Some scientific statements are unclear or incorrect.

➡ Band 6, because you answered the question but with unclear development.

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 5

• Clear paragraphing
• You use connectors (“On the other hand,” “For instance”).

Problems
• Many sentences don’t flow logically.
• Repetition of ideas.
• Linking is often mechanical or incorrect.
• The opinion paragraph is too short and does not logically connect to the main essay.
• Cohesion breaks due to grammar mistakes.

➡ Band 5 (sentences not fluently linked, some unclear relationships).

• other claim deforestation has more risk
• i strongly believe
• greenhouse gases generated by unlimited car using

• deforestations has more impact
• effect to our planet
• A lot of wildfires occurs
• it is on of the strongest impacts
• inhabiting organisms like a human

These errors appear in almost every sentence.

➡ Band 5:
“frequent grammatical errors and faulty complex structures.”

OVERALL: 5.0/5.5

Rewritten essay for BAND 7:

Some people argue that global warming is the most urgent environmental problem we face today, while others believe that deforestation has a more devastating impact. Both issues are serious, and this essay will discuss each view. However, I believe that climate change has a wider and more dangerous influence on our planet.

Those who think global warming is the most pressing problem point to several key reasons. First, greenhouse gases produced by car emissions and traditional energy production significantly increase carbon dioxide levels. In 2024 alone, more than 41 billion tons of CO₂ were released into the atmosphere, and this amount continues to rise by about 0.5% each year. As a result, global temperatures continue to increase. Another major concern is overpopulation. The world’s population is expected to reach almost 9 billion by 2037, and we already face shortages of food and water in many regions. Higher temperatures make it even more difficult to manage these limited resources.

On the other hand, supporters of the opposite view argue that deforestation has a more direct impact on daily life. Forests play a crucial role in producing oxygen and absorbing carbon dioxide, so cutting them down reduces air quality and increases the risk of respiratory diseases. Additionally, deforestation often leads to the destruction of habitats. Many species cannot survive anywhere else, and their loss contributes to a decline in biodiversity. Wildfires, many of which are caused by human activity, also destroy millions of hectares of forest every year and release large amounts of carbon dioxide into the air.

In my view, climate change and deforestation are connected problems, but global warming is more dangerous because it affects the entire planet. Rising temperatures cause extreme weather, rising sea levels and long-term changes that no single country can tackle alone.

In conclusion, although both global warming and deforestation have serious environmental consequences, global warming poses a more significant threat due to its global scale and long-lasting effects.
AI Feedback
🌸

Discuss both views and give your opinion. ESSAY: People have different views about whether global warming one of the negative impact of environment. Some argue that it is not crusial to dispute, while other people believe that disastrous affect of logging is more signaficant. This essay will examine both sides and explain why i support the idea that global warming has important to discuss. On the one hand, opponents of deforestation believe is disastrous problem. One common argument is that farmers cut down trees to create grazing land for livestock. As population grow, people consume more beef, milk, and other animal products. Another point is that many countries depend on wood exports for economic growth. Exporting more wood means realese CO2 and speeds up climate change. As a result, more CO2 stays in the atmosphere and warm the planet. One the other hand, there some people, as well as i, argue that global warming has more traumatic impact on natural environment. One main negatives is that temperatures rise, ice caps melt, causing sea level increase. Small island nations such as Maldives may disappear completely. This can also lead to extreme wheater, such as stronger hurricanes.These events destroy homes, damage crops, and reduce food supplies. Farmland might be flooding, and crops like maize can lost for the whole season, i believe that global warming puts nature and human life at great risk. In conclution, althought deforestation can cause disastrous problem, i belive that global warming has more traumatic impacts than deforestation. If we ignore global warming today, we will pay a heavy price tomorrow.

• Conclusion repeats rather than summarises clearly

➡ Band 6 TR, but weak because ideas lack depth.

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 5

❌ crops like maize can lost → can be lost
❌ One main negatives → One main negative effect

• “farmers cut down trees… people consume more beef” → the connection is unclear
• “traumatic impact on natural environment” → not appropriate
• “Farmland might flooding” → incorrect

Vague or too general vocabulary:
• “disastrous problem”
• “traumatic impacts”
• “heavy price tomorrow” (acceptable but slightly cliché)

“frequent inaccuracies in word choice and collocation; meaning may be unclear.”

This matches your writing.

4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5

Very frequent grammar errors:
• “which we facing right now”
• “other claim deforestation has more risk”

• “As population grow”
• “One the other hand, there some people”
• “temperatures rise, ice caps melt, causing sea level increase” (missing article)
• “maize can lost”
• “i belive”
• “conclution”

• Missing articles

• Fragments
• Run-on sentences

Exactly matches your essay.

Rewritten essay for BAND 7:

People have different views about whether global warming or deforestation is the most serious environmental problem we face today. Some argue that global warming is not a crucial issue, while others believe that the destructive effects of logging are more significant. This essay will examine both sides and explain why I believe that global warming is more urgent to address.

On the one hand, many people think that deforestation causes serious damage to the environment. One common argument is that farmers cut down trees to create grazing land for livestock. As the global population grows, the demand for beef, milk and other animal products increases, which leads to more forest destruction. Another argument is that many countries depend on wood exports for economic development. However, cutting and processing large amounts of wood releases carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, which contributes to climate change. As a result, fewer trees are available to absorb CO₂, and more carbon stays in the air, warming the planet.

On the other hand, many people, including myself, believe that global warming is a more serious problem. Rising temperatures cause extreme weather conditions such as floods, heatwaves and hurricanes. These events destroy homes, damage farmland and reduce food supplies. Low-lying island countries like the Maldives may completely disappear due to rising sea levels. Furthermore, higher temperatures can cause long periods of drought, making it difficult for farmers to grow essential crops such as maize. I believe that global warming threatens both human life and natural ecosystems worldwide.

In conclusion, although deforestation has many harmful effects, I believe that global warming has a greater impact because it influences the entire planet. If we ignore this problem now, the consequences in the future will be far more severe.
AI Feedback
🌸
🌸 Discuss both views Akramjonov Muslimbek31/12/1969, 16:00:00
Rubrik: While, some people consider Global Warming to be more pressing enviromental problem which we have at the moment, others belive that deforestation has more divastation impacts on our world. Disscuss both views and give your opinion. Essay: There is no doubt that deforestation and Global Warming currently are the most considerable issues. Some individuals are the opinion that Global warming is the most destructive problem, while others belive that detremental impacts of cutting down trees is more significant. This essay will discuss both of these views and explain why I agree with formef. On the one hand, there are some reasons why some individuals hold the view that deforestation is detremental issue. One common argument is that forests absorb carbon dioxyde and emit clear oxygen. An avarage tree, for instance, remove from 22 to 25 kilogramms of CO2 annualy. Another point is that forests are home for many animal species, and cutting down trees leads to extinction of animals, especially for those who are endangered. For example, one type of leopard in Taiwan became extinct due to logging. On the other hand, there are some people, as well as I, belive that Global Warming has much more devastative impact on natural world. Firstly, because of this issue, polar ices are melting 4 times faster than it was several decades ago. By this, global sea levels are rising. For instance, Venice is at the edge of flood, and rising of sea levels can destruct this miraculous city. Secondly, rising temperatures can cause extreme weather conditions, for example hurricanes and so on. I belive that Global Warming can cause more drammatic impacts for atmosphere and will cause respiratory problems in the short-term. To sum up, there is no doubt that Global Warming has much more distructful impacts than deforestation.

• Some arguments need more explanation and development.
• The conclusion is too short and doesn’t summarise the arguments.
• “I agree with formef” — unclear and incorrect.
• Some scientific claims need more accuracy.

Band 6 TR = addresses task but lacks precision and development.

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 6

Strengths
• Good paragraph structure
• Logical sequencing
• Clear topic sentences

Problems
• Linking words are basic and sometimes misused.
• Some ideas are not smoothly connected.
• Repeated “On the other hand” and “Firstly” without full cohesion.
• Sudden ending — conclusion is too short and unbalanced.

Band 6 descriptor: “cohesion may be faulty or mechanical” — this matches your writing.

3. Lexical Resource — Band 5

This is the weakest area, mainly due to misspellings and incorrect collocations.

❌ Frequent spelling errors:
• detremental → detrimental
• formef → unclear
• dioxyde → dioxide
• avarage → average
• kilogramms → kilograms
• annualy → annually
• belive → believe
• devastative → devastating
• distructful → destructive
• drammatic → dramatic

❌ Incorrect or unnatural collocations:
• “Global Warming has more devastative impact” → devastating
• “destruct this miraculous city” → destroy this historic city
• “respiratory problems in the short-term” (unclear in context)

“frequent inaccuracies in word choice and spelling errors cause some difficulty for the reader.”

4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5.5

Frequent grammar mistakes:
• Global Warming currently are → is
• Some individuals are the opinion → are of the opinion
• detrimental impacts of cutting down trees is → are
• I agree with formef → unclear meaning
• One common argument is that forests absorb carbon dioxide and emit clear oxygen.
(“clear oxygen” is not natural English; just “oxygen”)
• were melting 4 times faster… → are melting
• can lost → can be lost
• distructful impacts → incorrect

Sentence structure is understandable but contains too many basic errors for Band 6+.

OVERALL: 5.5

Rewritten essay for BAND 7:

There is no doubt that both deforestation and global warming are major environmental problems today. Some people believe that global warming is the most destructive issue, while others argue that the harmful effects of cutting down trees are more serious. This essay will discuss both views and explain why I agree with the former opinion.

On the one hand, many people think that deforestation is a highly damaging environmental issue. Forests absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen, which helps maintain clean air. An average tree, for example, can remove about 22 to 25 kilograms of CO₂ every year. In addition, forests are home to many species of animals, and large-scale logging can lead to habitat loss and even extinction. For instance, one type of leopard in Taiwan became extinct because its forest habitat was destroyed.

On the other hand, there are people, including myself, who believe that global warming has a much more devastating impact on the natural world. Firstly, rising temperatures are causing ice caps to melt almost four times faster than they did several decades ago. This contributes to rising sea levels. Venice, for example, is currently at high risk of flooding, and higher sea levels could eventually destroy this historic city. Secondly, global warming leads to extreme weather conditions such as hurricanes, heatwaves and severe storms. These events damage homes, farmland and food supplies. I believe that global warming threatens both the atmosphere and human health.

In conclusion, although deforestation has serious negative effects, I believe that global warming is a more significant threat because its consequences are global and long-lasting.
AI Feedback
🌸
🌸 Sevinch Turopova31/12/1969, 16:00:00
Some people argue that global warming is the most urgent environmental issue we face today, while others believe that deforestation causes more serious long-term damage. Both problems pose significant threats to our planet, and I subscribe to the former idea and will explain why. There are some reasons why some people hold the view that cutting down trees can be detrimental to our globe. First of all, deforestation destroys the natural habitats of countless species leading to a decline in biodiversity and even an extinction of rare animals. Forests also play a important role in maintaining the balance of the atmosphere by absorbing carbon dioxide and producing oxygen. When large areas of trees are removed, carbon emissions increase and contribute to climate unstability. In addition, deforestation results in soil erosion and land degradation, which reduce agricultural productivity and make communities more vulnerable to natural disasters such as floods and landslides. These shows why many people consider deforestation as a highly destructive environmental problem. On the other hand, opponents of deforestation, as well as I, claim that raising the temperature of the earth’s surface has huge devastating impacts on our environment. Global warming leads to extreme weather events including heatwaves, droughts and storms, all of which seriously affect human health and the natural world. Rising temperatures also cause the melting of polar ice caps leading to higher sea levels and putting millions of people living in coastal regions at risk. Moreover, climate change distrupts global food production making it harder for developing countries to ensure food security. Marine creatures are also suffering, as warmer oceans damage coral reefs and change a behaviour of fish species. In conclusion, although deforestation has serious environmental consequences, I believe that global warming is a more pressing issue due to its widespread and long-lasting impacts. That is why governments and individuals should take urgent action to reduce emissions and protect our planet for future generations!
Word count: 319
Teacher Feedback
1. Task Response — Band 7

Strengths
• You address both sides fully
• Your opinion is clear and consistent
• All main ideas are explained logically
• You include consequences, examples, and specific impacts
• Good balance between paragraphs

Small issues
• A few statements are slightly unclear or unnatural:
• “climate unstability” → climate instability
• “These shows why…” → subject–verb agreement error
• Stronger examples would improve this further.

Despite these, the task is well-answered → Band 7.

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 7

Strengths
• Clear organisation
• Strong paragraph flow
• Good use of connectors: first of all, in addition, moreover, on the other hand
• Logical progression from causes → effects

Minor issues
• A few overly long sentences can reduce clarity
• Conclusion ends with an exclamation mark, which is inappropriate for academic writing

Still, it meets Band 7 requirements.

3. Lexical Resource — Band 6.5

This is the area holding your score down.

Strengths
• Good range of topic vocabulary:
biodiversity, carbon emissions, soil erosion, long-lasting impacts, coastal regions, food security
• Collocations mostly accurate
• Clear meaning throughout

Problems (preventing Band 7+)
• Several incorrect or unnatural phrases:
• “subscribe to the former idea” → correct meaning but slightly unnatural
• “a important role” → should be “an important role”
• “climate unstability” → instability
• “distrupts” → disrupts
• “change a behaviour of fish species” → change the behaviour of fish species
• Occasional awkward wording:
• “These shows why” → This shows why / These examples show why

Your vocabulary range is good, but accuracy and collocation control are not fully Band 7.

4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 6

Strengths
• Good variety of complex structures
• Most sentences are clear
• Some natural conditional and relative clauses

Errors that reduce the band:
• Article mistakes: “a important role”, “a extinction”
• Singular/plural agreement: “these shows”
• Wrong verb forms: “distrupts”
• Incorrect prepositions: “change a behaviour of”
• Slightly informal final sentence

Band 7 requires frequent error-free sentences, but you still have noticeable errors in every paragraph.

OVERALL: 6.5

Rewritten essay for BAND 7.5 - analyse it:

Some people argue that global warming is the most urgent environmental issue we face today, while others believe that deforestation causes more serious long-term damage. Both problems pose significant risks to our planet; however, I support the view that global warming is the more pressing concern.

On the one hand, many people believe that cutting down trees has severe consequences for the environment. Forests play an essential role in regulating the atmosphere by absorbing carbon dioxide and releasing oxygen. An average tree, for example, can remove around 22 to 25 kilograms of CO₂ each year. In addition, forests provide habitats for countless species, and large-scale logging often leads to a loss of biodiversity and even the extinction of endangered animals. The extinction of the Formosan clouded leopard in Taiwan is one such example. Furthermore, deforestation contributes to soil erosion and land degradation, which reduce agricultural productivity and increase communities’ vulnerability to floods and landslides. These points show why many people see deforestation as a highly destructive environmental problem.

On the other hand, I believe that global warming has an even more devastating impact on the natural world. Rising temperatures intensify extreme weather events such as heatwaves, droughts and powerful storms, all of which threaten human health and ecosystems. Higher temperatures also accelerate the melting of polar ice caps, leading to rising sea levels. As a result, low-lying coastal areas, including cities like Venice, face an increased risk of flooding. Moreover, climate change disrupts global food production by damaging crops and altering growing conditions, making it more difficult for developing countries to maintain food security. Marine life is also under threat, as warmer oceans harm coral reefs and affect the migratory patterns and behaviour of many fish species.

In conclusion, although deforestation has serious environmental consequences, I believe that global warming is a more urgent issue due to its global scale and long-lasting effects. For this reason, both governments and individuals must take immediate action to reduce carbon emissions and protect our planet for future generations.
AI Feedback
🌸
THE WINNER TAKES IT ALL

Both sides are important and we should consider them carefully. This essay will look into both views and explains why I believe that climate change is more important than cutting down forests. On the one hand, there is a belief that cutting down forests is also important problem. Forest play a crutial role in absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen. When trees are cut for agriculture or logging this is bad for nature. Deforestation has important impact on local people. The may lose food shelter and culture. Cutting down trees leads to loss of plants and animals which cannot survive aywhere else. Despite these arguments, I believe that global warming is most significant issue. Rising temperatures is often cause extreme weather such as floods, heatvaves and hurricanes. These weather not only danger for people but also cause serious damage to agriculture and housing. Also climate change cause to melting polar ice caps and rising sea levels. Climate change is global issue that no one can solve alone. In conclusion although both sides have strong arguments , I would like to say that global warming effect the entire planet, while cutting down trees causes serous effect on individuals. In my view both problems are connected and it is difficult to say that one is more important than the other. But we need to tale a action quickly on global warming.

1. Task Response — Band 6

• You addressed both views

(“This is bad for nature” → too simple, needs explanation)
• Several statements are too general or unclear
• Conclusion contradicts your opinion a little (“difficult to say which is more important”)
• No examples
• Some scientific statements are inaccurate (“lose food shelter and culture” → what culture?)

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 5

Strengths
• Clear paragraphing
• Basic logical structure
• Linking phrases such as “On the one hand” and “Despite these arguments”

Problems
• Weak linking inside paragraphs
• Sentences do not flow smoothly
• Some ideas are disconnected or repeated
• Many cohesion errors:
• “This is bad for nature” (too simple → unclear link)
• “The may lose food shelter and culture” (meaning unclear)
• “Climate change cause to melting…” (grammar breaks cohesion)

Band 5 descriptors include:
“sentences are not fluently linked to each other”
This matches your essay.

3. Lexical Resource — Band 5

Lexical resource is one of the weakest areas in this essay.

• You attempt topic-related vocabulary

Problems (frequent and serious)
• Incorrect word forms: “climate change issue”, “melting polar ice caps”, “crutial”, “serous effect”, “tale a action”
• Wrong collocations:
• “cutting down of trees is having more dangerous impact”
• “global warming effect the entire planet”
• “climate change cause to melting”

• “They may lose food shelter and culture”
• “These weather”

“frequent inaccuracies in word choice and collocation”
This matches your writing exactly.

4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5

Grammar has many errors that affect meaning.

Examples:
• “climate change issue is more urgent” → missing article
• “cutting down of trees is having more dangerous impact” → wrong tense + wrong article
• “Forest play a crutial role” → subject–verb agreement
• “absorb carbon dioxide and produce oxygen” → correct but needs “forests absorb…”
• “The may lose food shelter and culture” → grammar incorrect
• “Rising temperatures is often cause extreme weather” → subject–verb agreement
• “These weather not only danger for people” → incorrect structure
• “Climate change cause to melting” → incorrect grammar
• “global warming effect the entire planet” → should be “affects”
• “tale a action quickly” → “take action quickly”

Band 5 descriptor:
“frequent grammatical errors may cause some difficulty for the reader.”
This fits your essay.

OVERALL: 5.0/5.5

Rewritten essay for BAND 7:

Some people think that climate change is the most urgent environmental problem today, while others argue that cutting down trees has a more harmful impact. Both sides have strong arguments, and this essay will discuss them. However, I believe that climate change is the more serious issue.

On the one hand, many people think that deforestation is a major problem. Forests play a crucial role in absorbing carbon dioxide and producing oxygen, so when large areas of trees are cut down, air quality becomes worse. Deforestation also has a strong effect on local communities. People who live near forests may lose important natural resources, such as food, shelter and traditional plants used in medicine. Furthermore, forests are home to many unique species of animals and plants. When habitats are destroyed, many species cannot survive anywhere else, which leads to a loss of biodiversity.

On the other hand, I believe that climate change is even more urgent. Rising temperatures often cause extreme weather events such as floods, heatwaves and hurricanes. These events not only put people’s lives at risk but also damage agriculture, homes and infrastructure. In addition, climate change contributes to the melting of polar ice caps, which leads to rising sea levels. This threatens coastal cities and may force millions of people to relocate. Climate change is a global problem that affects every country, and it cannot be solved by one nation alone.

In conclusion, although deforestation has serious consequences for wildlife and local communities, I believe that climate change is the more important issue because it affects the entire planet. As both problems are connected, we must take action quickly to reduce global warming and protect our environment.
AI Feedback

1. Task Response — Band 6

Strengths:
• You addressed both views
• You gave an opinion
• You attempted explanations

Problems:
• Many arguments are unclear or inaccurate
• Some explanations are not logically developed
Examples:
• “trees are cleared our ecosystem” — unclear
• “contributes to rising greenhouse gases, alternatively to our overall well-being” — incorrect logic
• “relating to our breath” — unnatural and unclear
• No real examples
• Conclusion is weak and grammatically inaccurate

Band 6 TR requires “some development but with clarity issues.”
You match that level.

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 5.5

Problems:
• Many sentences do not follow logically
• Cohesive devices are misused / unnatural:
• “alternatively to our well-being”
• “practice has been showing,”
• Topic sentences are unclear
• Paragraphs are loosely organised; ideas jump
• Frequent grammar issues break cohesion

Band 6 requires “clear progression with some lapses.”
You have frequent lapses, so this is Band 5.5.

3. Lexical Resource — Band 5

Your vocabulary range is LARGE, but accuracy is weak.
This is the biggest problem stopping you from Band 6–7.

Below are repeated lexical issues:

❌ Incorrect / unnatural collocations:
• “more pressing environmental problem which we have at the moment”
→ “the most pressing environmental problem we currently face”
• “temperature change might causing”
• “may has far more effects”
• “global heating” (not used in academic English; use global warming)
• “forests which are prone to situated in a destroying zone”
• “vast numerous of species who are habitat in there”
• “trees are cleared our ecosystem”
• “practice of deforestation released the carbon”
• “related to our breath”
• “rising level of sea”
• “practice has been showing”
• “potential dryland result from highest temperature”
• “outweighed and could has far more detrimental consequences”

These are unnatural, incorrect, or scientifically inaccurate.

Lexical Resource Band 5 descriptors include:
• “frequent inaccuracies in word choice”
• “inappropriate lexical items”
• “lack of collocational control”

Your essay matches these features exactly.

4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5

There are grammar errors in almost every sentence:

❌ Examples:
• “global warming to be more pressing environmental problem”
→ “a more pressing environmental problem”
• “temperature change might causing detrimental influences”
→ “might cause”
• “deforestation might has enormous effects”
→ “might have”
• “a vast numerous of species who are habitat in there”
→ incorrect structure
• “Practice of deforestation released the carbon”
• “global warming cause more issue”
• “warming is outweighed and could has…”

Band 5 grammar descriptors include:
• “frequent errors”
• “limited control”
• “complex sentences often faulty”

This fits your essay.

OVERALL: 5.5

Rewritten essay for Band 7:

Nowadays, it is undeniable that both global warming and deforestation have severe effects on the environment. Some people believe that rising temperatures are a more urgent problem, while others argue that cutting down trees has a greater impact. This essay will discuss both views, and I will explain why I believe that global warming causes more serious issues than deforestation.

On the one hand, many people think that deforestation is the main environmental problem. One major reason is the loss of biodiversity. Forests are home to thousands of species, and when large areas of trees are destroyed, many animals lose their habitats. This can lead to a decline in their population and even extinction. In addition, deforestation contributes to climate change. Trees absorb carbon dioxide, and when they are cut down, more greenhouse gases remain in the atmosphere. As a result, air quality worsens, and people may face more breathing-related health problems.

On the other hand, I believe that global warming is an even more serious issue. The most obvious impact of rising temperatures is the increase in sea levels. As the planet becomes warmer, ice sheets and glaciers melt, adding more water to the oceans. This threatens coastal cities and puts millions of people at risk of flooding. Furthermore, higher temperatures increase the frequency of droughts, especially in dry regions. Hot weather causes faster evaporation of water resources, which results in water shortages and damaged agricultural land.

In conclusion, although deforestation has harmful effects on wildlife and the climate, I believe that global warming is a more critical problem. It leads to rising sea levels, extreme weather conditions and severe consequences for both people and the environment.

Rubrik: Some students take a year off between school and university, to work or to travel. Do the advantages outweigh the disadvantages Essay:
It is true that these days a gap year is taken before going to higher education for making a trip or engaging in employment by many school leavers. While this trend can bring some immediate drawbacks, I am convinced that it is more likely to have long-term benefits for their lives. On the one hand, year off had brought some drawbacks. For instance, managing money is valuable skill which should be learned from young age. To sum up, despite some negative aspects, I believe that the benefits of this gap year are more significant for the reasons aforementioned.

This essay will look into both views and explain why I concur with the beneficial side of it.  The majority of students taking a gap year is rising, and in this period, downsides may appear. First, it can cause losing study habits. After a long break, it might be severe for them to return to an academic routine, which might affect their performance in university. Furthermore, teenagers in their age group want to travel more, and it allows them to learn about different cultures and develop important life skills, such as self-confidence and communication abilities. These skills are very useful in the university and in the future workplace.  In conclusion, despite some disadvantages, the advantages are far more significant. And the students who take a year off often become more mature and experienced at the beginning of the courses.

🌸 Ozodbek31/12/1969, 16:00:00
Nowadays people are facing a myriad of problems with the environment, and at this moment most people's opinions are divided into two groups. Some individuals are of the opinion that global warming is a more pressing problem, while others believe that deforestation is very harmful. This essay will discuss both views and conclude with personal perspectives, however I believe that global warming cause more issue than deforestation. There are some reasons why some people hold the opinion that deforestation is the main problem. The first reason might be cutting down the trees that can filter the polluted air to fresh air, but we should consider that most of the polluted air is filtered by the underwater plants like algae. Despite this reason, cutting down the trees might affect pollution. How? Because the dust goes up when the trees are falling down and can pollute a little city and may be the cause of diseases, like blurring the eyesight or problems with the breathing ability. Furthermore, there is a belief that global warming is an issue as big as deforestation but worse. We know that global warming is caused by deforestation too, and there are some reasons too. Cutting down the trees and constructing new buildings instead has effects on the environment. The ultraviolet light that comes from the sun falls to the ground and goes back to the upper surface, but when the light comes and falls on the asphalt, the substance in the asphalt begins to heat up, and the cut trees can't absorb at least half of the ultraviolet light. Looking at both arguments, it's clear that global warming causes more problems than deforestation, but we can clearly see that cutting down the trees is one of the reasons for global warming.

Rewritten essay for Band 7.0 - analyse it:

Nowadays, people face many environmental problems, and opinions about the most serious one are divided. Some people believe that global warming is the most pressing issue, while others argue that deforestation is more harmful. This essay will discuss both views, but I believe that global warming causes more problems than deforestation.

Those who think deforestation is the main issue point out several negative effects. First, trees help improve air quality by absorbing carbon dioxide and releasing oxygen. When forests are cut down, the air becomes more polluted, which may lead to health problems such as breathing difficulties. In addition, deforestation increases the amount of dust in the air and reduces natural shade, making cities hotter and less comfortable for people to live in.

On the other hand, many people believe that global warming is a more serious problem. Rising temperatures lead to a range of dangerous consequences, including melting ice caps, rising sea levels and more frequent natural disasters. These changes threaten not only wildlife but also human populations living in coastal areas. Furthermore, global warming is made worse by deforestation itself, because trees that normally absorb carbon dioxide are removed. As a result, more greenhouse gases stay in the atmosphere, trapping heat and increasing global temperatures.

In conclusion, although deforestation is a serious environmental issue, I believe that global warming is more critical because it leads to natural disasters and affects the entire planet. Since deforestation contributes to global warming, both issues need attention, but rising temperatures should be our main concern.

🌸 Farida31/12/1969, 16:00:00
There has been a noticeable rise in the number of students who choose to continue their college or university studies in a foreign country. While this trend can involve certain financial and emotional difficulties, I believe its advantages are far more significant. It is true that studying overseas can create financial presure. International tuition fees and living expenses are usually high, meaning that families may need to make significant sacrifices. Some students even rely on part-time jobs, which can add extra stress. Additionally, cultural and emotional difficulties are common among international students. They often face unfamiliar social norms, language barriers, and feelings of homesickness, all of which can affect their well-being during the first months abroad. Despite these issues, the advantages of studying abroad are far more significant. One major benefit is the opportunitiy to develop strong language skills. Living in a country where the target language is used daily allows students to gain natural fluency, something that is difficult to achieve through classroom study alone. Moreover, foreign universities often provide access to high-quality education, modern facilities, and innovative teaching methods. Students can later bring this advanced knowledge back to their home country, helping improve local industries, education, and research. In this way, studying abroad not only benefits individuals but also contributes to national development. Overall,while the financial and emotional challenges of studying in another country are undeniable, the long-term benefits such as improved language proficiency and access to world-class education are far more influential.

Rewritten essay for Band 7:

Global environmental problems have become a major concern today. Some people believe that rising temperatures are the most serious issue, while others argue that large-scale deforestation is more critical. This essay will discuss both views and explain why I believe that global warming is the greater threat.

Those who think deforestation is more serious point out several important effects. First, cutting down trees leads to higher levels of air pollution. Trees absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen, so when forests disappear, air quality becomes worse and people may face more health problems. Second, deforestation affects our water sources. Trees act like natural sponges, collecting rainwater and helping to filter it. Without them, water supplies become less reliable and may not be clean enough for drinking or agriculture.

On the other hand, many people, including myself, see global warming as an even more urgent problem. One major reason is that rising temperatures lead to more natural disasters. For example, the melting of icebergs raises sea levels, which can cause floods and destroy the habitats of both animals and humans. Another concern is the erosion of the ozone layer, which is damaged by carbon emissions produced by human activity. This increases the risk of skin cancer and other health problems. Global warming also affects plants and ecosystems, disrupting food production and threatening biodiversity.

In conclusion, although deforestation is a serious issue, I believe that global warming is an even bigger threat. Its impact on natural disasters, human health and the environment makes it the most urgent environmental problem facing our world today.
