While this trend has some negatives, I would argue that the positives are more substantial. On the one hand, studying abroad at a higher educational institution may involve several drawbacks. The most common disadvantage is the financial cost. For instance, students could encounter with high cost of tuition fees, accommodation expanses and travel cost which can create financial pressure for students. And sometimes, most of the families could not be prepared overpay amount of money for studying abroad because of their limited budgets. Additionally, majority of students claim that another major negative of this is adapting to a new lifestyle which is prone to entail some obstacles. Studying in another country may lead to feelings of loneliness or cultural shock, especially for those who struggle to adapt to unfamiliar social norms. Finally it is likely that potential candidates who are aid to study overseas may not be familiar with the local policies. In spite of above arguments, I would argue that these drawbacks are outweighed by the advantages. One of the greatest positive aspects is the opportunity for personal growth. Students who attend foreign universities can obtain unique experiences and exposed to higher-quality education. Many of Western countries are remarkably well known with their innovative teaching methods. This environmental can significantly enhance their knowledge and practical skills. In addition, living in another country allows young people built themselves and being independent. Such experiences strengthen key soft skills including problem-solving, communication, which is valued by CEO. To conclude, there are a number of downsides associated with studying in a foreign country, including financial strain and issues with adapting to a new environment. Nevertheless, these drawbacks are relatively minor compared to the positives, such as the chance for personal development and the accelerate of knowledge. Therefore, the advantages of this clearly outweigh the negatives.

Your essay for Band 7.0 - analyse it:

Some people argue that global warming is the most serious environmental problem we face today, while others believe that deforestation has a greater impact on the world. This essay will discuss both views, but I believe that cutting down trees is a more significant issue.

Many people consider global warming to be the most urgent challenge because its effects are already visible across the planet. One major concern is the rapid melting of the polar ice caps. As global temperatures rise, huge amounts of ice in the Arctic and Antarctic melt, which causes sea levels to increase. This puts coastal cities at risk of flooding and may force millions of people to relocate in the future. In addition, global warming has a direct impact on human health. Heatwaves have become more frequent, leading to heat-related illnesses and higher death rates, especially among the elderly and children.

However, I believe that deforestation is an even more serious problem. Trees play a vital role in producing oxygen and absorbing carbon dioxide, which helps regulate the Earth’s climate. When large areas of forest are destroyed, air quality becomes worse and global warming accelerates. Moreover, deforestation threatens countless species by destroying their natural habitats. Another important point is that trees act as natural carbon sinks. If they are cut down, more carbon dioxide remains in the atmosphere, trapping heat and increasing global temperatures.

In conclusion, although global warming is a major environmental issue, I believe that deforestation is more significant because of its direct impact on climate regulation, biodiversity and the quality of life on our planet.

EDITED VERSION.
Rubric: More and more students are choosing to study at colleges and universities in a foreign country. Do the benefits of studying abroad outweigh the drawbacks?

In this era of globalization, many students want to study in a country abroad. This decision has some upsides, but also some downsides. In my opinion, the benefits are a little stronger than the drawbacks.
One the one hand, this situation has disadvantages. one big problem is that studying abroad can be expensive. Tuition and living costs must be paid, and many families cannot afford this. Another problem is homesickness. Students might be affected by loneliness because their family is far away. Sometimes it is difficult to adapt to a new culture. Students can be faced cultural shock. When students move to a new country or environment with different traditions, language, and lifestyle they can fell confusion and stress. 
One the other hand, studying abroad also has advantages. First of all, good education can be received in other countries. Many useful subjects are taught better there, so students can learn more. Also, a new culture and language canbe learned. This might help students become more openminded and more confident. Furthermore foreign county university's diploma can help to students find the better job rather than local university's diploma.
In conclution, even though there are some problems with studying abroad, i think the advantages are more important. Students can get better knowledge and a new experience, so the benifits outweigh the drawbacks.Overall, studying another country can open new doors for students and help them grow personally and academically. It often becomes a life changing opportunity that shapes their future in a positive way.

Some individuals are of the opinion that studying in a foreign country is unnecessary for students, while others believe that going abroad for education is a very essential part of modern learning. Some people think that living and studying overseas only make students confused and stressed, whereas others claim that it helps them improve their knowledge more deeper. On the one hand, there is a belief that foreign study bring more harm than good. Many parents argue that students already face many challenges at home, so sending them abroad is unfair and too expensive for families. It is undeniable that the most significant benefit of staying in their own country is that learners can have more comfortable life with family support. However some people says that studying abroad causes loneliness and even mental problem to students, but this argument is sometimes exaggerated. On the other hand, I would argue that these disadvantages are outweigted by the positive aspects. Advocates of studying overseas argue that it improves independence, language skills and practise of communication. For example, without living in another country, students often cannot learn foreign languages very quick. While studying abroad can be difficult sometimes, I am convinced that these positive aspects are more important for long-term success. Those who advocate international education believe that it prepares students for global careers and future opportunities. In conclusion, although both sides have strong argumments, I would like to say that studying in a foreign country plays a main role in students education. Summing up, there is no doubt that some challenges exist, but avoiding international study completly would be a mistake for many young people.
Word count: 273
Teacher Feedback
1. Task Response — Band 6

Strengths
• You discuss both sides
• You give your opinion clearly
• Your position is consistent

Problems
• Some ideas are underdeveloped or unclear
• Some arguments are repeated
• “Studying abroad causes confusion and stress” → no explanation
• Conclusion restates ideas but doesn’t summarise well
• Some statements are imprecise (“improve their knowledge more deeper”)

➡ You answer the question, but not fully or clearly enough for Band 7.

2. Coherence & Cohesion — Band 6

Strengths
• Paragraphing is logical
• Connectors “on the one hand / on the other hand” are used correctly

Problems
• Linking inside paragraphs is weak
• Some sentences break cohesion because of grammar errors
• Paragraph 1: ideas jump without smooth transitions
• “However some people says…” → grammatical error + weak link

➡ Structure is good, but the flow is not smooth enough for Band 7.

3. Lexical Resource — Band 5–5.5

This is the biggest issue.

❌ Incorrect or unnatural expressions:
• “foreign study bring” → foreign study brings
• “knowledge more deeper” → learn more deeply / gain deeper knowledge
• “practise of communication” → communication skills
• “main role in students education”
• “completly”
• “argumments”
• “very essential” → “essential” is enough
• “makes students confused” → unnatural collocation
• “causes mental problem” → mental health problems

Many of your phrases sound translated, not natural English.

Band 7 requires precise, natural collocations. Your essay has too many inaccuracies.

4. Grammar Range & Accuracy — Band 5

Frequent errors:
• Subject–verb agreement:
“foreign study bring”, “some people says”
• Articles missing: “students education”, “have more comfortable life”
• Wrong word forms: “more deeper”, “main role”, “practise of communication”
• Repeated punctuation errors
• Fragmented sentences

You attempt complex structures, but there are errors in almost every sentence, which is Band 5.

OVERALL: 5.5

Rewritten essay for BAND 7:
Some individuals believe that studying in a foreign country is unnecessary for students, while others argue that international education plays an essential role in modern learning. Some people claim that living and studying overseas can confuse or stress students, whereas others think it helps them gain deeper knowledge and important life skills. This essay will discuss both views and explain why I support the latter opinion.

On the one hand, many people believe that studying abroad brings more disadvantages than benefits. Parents often argue that students already face many challenges at home, and sending them overseas is both expensive and emotionally difficult. It is true that staying in their own country allows students to enjoy family support and live more comfortably. Some people also claim that studying abroad can cause loneliness or stress, especially for teenagers who are not used to living independently. However, these concerns are sometimes exaggerated and depend largely on the individual student.

On the other hand, I believe that the advantages of studying abroad outweigh the drawbacks. Supporters of international education argue that it builds independence, improves language proficiency and strengthens communication skills. For example, students who live in another country usually learn a foreign language much faster than those who study it at home. Moreover, studying abroad exposes young people to different cultures and perspectives, which prepares them for global careers and increases their future opportunities. Although adapting to a new environment can be challenging, these experiences help students grow both academically and personally.

In conclusion, although studying abroad can present certain difficulties, I believe it plays an important role in students’ education. Avoiding international study completely would mean missing valuable opportunities for personal development, cultural understanding and long-term success.

Nowadays its undeniable that an overwhelming majority of students opt to study at educational institutions abroad. Some individuals think that studying overseas country has some distinct advantages whereas others claim that this trend may carry more downside. While this trend has some negatives, i would argue that positives of this are more substantial. On the one hand, study abroad at higher education may involve several drawbacks. The most common disadvantage is in a financial cost. Most of the students who are studying in a foreign country experience with a additional expenditures such as insures, taxes and even tuition fees. Additionally, another major negatives of this is adapting to lifestyle. It is likely that you may are not familiar with culture, tradition, social norms and even with policy. However, I would argue that these drawbacks are outweighed by the upsides. One of the greatest positive aspect is the opportunity for personal growth. Who are studying abroad could obtain unique experience and practical skills, alternatively, it can lead to expanding sociability of students. Furthermore, it might be beneficial to gain general knowledge about country. Explore history of the country and neglecting significant part of region's heritage could broaden horizon. To conclude, there are number of downsides associated with study in a foreign country, including financial expenses and issues with adapting with environment atmosphere. Nevertheless these drawbacks are relatively minor compared to the positives, such as chance to personal development and enhancing general knowledge. Therefore advantages of this clearly outweigh the negatives

Nowadays, it is becoming popular among students choosing study at colleges and universities which located abroad. Moreover, studying abroad is very difficult for some people who are used to living in comfortable conditions when all facilities situated in one step from them. Students entering the university should clearly understand how they will communicate with foreigners and build new relationships withe them. Students who are introverted, probably, will feel uncomfortable themselves surrounded by new people and live conditions. One the other hand, studying abroad is has more opportunities to see the world and build new relationships which can strongly upside down your life. The most significant thing for people today is building the relationships that will help them in the future from getting a job to moving in new country and starting new life. The second strong benefit is traveling. While we are young we have more leisure time than when we start our own families. This is valuable opportunity to travel and discover a new world and get to know new cultures which helps you to boarder you knowledge horizons. In conclusion, despite the fact that topic is unique and has disadvantages such as high level of tuition fee and can be inconveniently for some people, advantages like a traveling and boarder your knowledge horizons carry more benefits for students future.

User Guide

For Students

1. Enter your name in the Your name field.

2. Write your essay in the Write your essay box.

3. Click Submit to save your essay to the cloud.

4. You can edit your essay by clicking the Edit button next to it.

5. To download your essay as a PDF, click Download PDF.

For Teachers

1. Enter the secret teacher key in the Teacher Key field and click Enter to access teacher mode.

2. Once in teacher mode, you can edit any essay, highlight important sections, underline useful phrases, or strike through errors.

3. You can also add or edit feedback by clicking Add/Edit Feedback next to an essay.

4. The teacher key is confidential. If you need the key, please contact Maqsudjon Polatov personally to request it.
